articles.headline: Rajya Sabha polls for 37 seats across states today
articles.summary: Hectic political maneuvering is underway for Rajya Sabha elections on 11 seats in Bihar, Odisha, and Haryana, while 26 candidates have already been elected unopposed. The NDA aims to expand its presence in the 234-member house, where it currently holds 134 seats. Opposition parties have moved legislators to resorts in multiple states to prevent potential cross-voting and horse-trading.

articles.headline: Two arrested for bribing Odisha Congress legislators
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy CM DK Shivakumar alleged that two men were arrested for attempting to bribe Odisha Congress MLAs staying at a Bengaluru resort. The individuals reportedly offered ₹5 crore each to influence votes in the upcoming Rajya Sabha election. Shivakumar claimed this was an 'Operation Lotus' attempt by the BJP. Blank cheques and other materials were seized by the Bidadi police.

articles.headline: Assembly bypolls for Bagalkote and Davangere on April 9
articles.summary: The ECI announced by-elections for the Bagalkot and Davangere South assembly seats in Karnataka following the deaths of senior Congress leaders HY Meti and Shamanur Shivashankarappa. Polling will occur on April 9 with counting on May 4. The Model Code of Conduct is now in force. Both the ruling Congress and opposition BJP have begun internal consultations for potential candidates.

articles.headline: Kerala to vote in single phase on April 9
articles.summary: The ECI announced that Kerala will vote for its 140-member assembly in a single phase on April 9. The contest involves the ruling LDF, the Congress-led UDF, and the BJP-led NDA. Key issues include the Sabarimala gold theft case and human-wildlife conflict. CM Pinarayi Vijayan will contest from Dharmadam. In 2021, the LDF won 99 seats, marking a historic second consecutive term.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu parties welcome April 23 poll date
articles.summary: The DMK, AIADMK, and other parties in Tamil Nadu welcomed the ECI’s announcement of a single-phase assembly election on April 23. The DMK expressed confidence in its welfare schemes, while the AIADMK cited law and order concerns as a basis for dislodging the current government. Counting for the 234 seats will begin on May 4 alongside other polling states.

articles.headline: Seat-sharing talks in NDA for Tamil Nadu soon
articles.summary: AIADMK chief Edappadi K Palaniswami announced that seat-sharing negotiations for the NDA in Tamil Nadu will be finalized soon. The alliance includes the AIADMK, BJP, AMMK, and PMK. Palaniswami noted that nominations start on March 30, providing sufficient time to determine candidates. He also dismissed DMK's comments on minority welfare as election-season rhetoric.

articles.headline: West Bengal's 6 million voters await status clarity
articles.summary: Uncertainty surrounds 6 million voters in West Bengal whose status remains under 'adjudication' following a special intensive revision of electoral rolls. The Trinamool Congress has criticized the process as a disenfranchisement attempt. ECI officials expect the first batch of supplementary rolls to be released this week, ahead of the April 6 nomination deadline for the first phase.

articles.headline: Bypolls for Baramati assembly seat on April 23
articles.summary: The ECI announced a by-election for Maharashtra's Baramati assembly seat, which fell vacant after the death of Deputy CM Ajit Pawar in a plane crash. Polling will occur on April 23. While BJP leaders aim for an unopposed election through consensus, they expressed readiness for a contest. Rohit Pawar of NCP(SP) suggested the family should allow Sunetra Pawar to be elected unopposed.

articles.headline: Mamata hikes DA and honorariums for priests
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee announced a hike in Dearness Allowance for state employees and increased monthly honorariums for mosque muezzins and Hindu priests. The announcement came an hour before the ECI released the poll schedule. Opposition leaders called the move a 'desperate last-minute poll gimmick,' while employee organizations welcomed the relief after a three-year agitation.

articles.headline: AAP releases first list of candidates for Assam
articles.summary: The Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) released its first list of 14 candidates for the upcoming Assam assembly elections. The list includes nominees for constituencies such as Naoboicha and Gohpur. The announcement comes as part of the party's push to expand its southern and northeastern footprint following the ECI's declaration of a single-phase election in Assam on April 9.

articles.headline: Five more arrested in Bengal political clashes
articles.summary: Police arrested five more individuals following clashes between TMC and BJP workers near the home of state minister Shashi Panja. The violence occurred ahead of PM Modi's rally at Brigade Parade Ground. The BJP has petitioned the ECI, alleging that Central Armed Police Forces were not present despite a substantial deployment to ensure safety during the sensitive pre-election period.

articles.headline: WB government transfers IPS officer after protocol row
articles.summary: The West Bengal government transferred Siliguri Police Commissioner C Sudhakar to the Intelligence Branch. The move follows a Union Home Ministry inquiry into a protocol row during President Droupadi Murmu’s visit to the state on March 7. The President had reportedly expressed disappointment over the handling of a Santhal community event and tribal development issues in the state.

articles.headline: Om Birla raises concern over conduct of MPs
articles.summary: Speaker Om Birla wrote to political party leaders expressing grave concern over the conduct of some MPs in the House and Parliament Complex. He criticized the display of banners and placards, as well as the behavior of members within the chamber, stating that the dignity of parliamentary democracy is being compromised. He urged party leaders to ensure high standards of discipline.

articles.headline: Amit Shah: Congress siphoned health budget in Assam
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah accused the previous Congress regime in Assam of pocketing ₹150 crore annually from the state's healthcare budget for 15 years. Speaking in Guwahati, Shah alleged funds meant for nutrition and 'anganwadi' centres were siphoned off. The Congress responded by calling the Home Minister's remarks a conspiracy and accused the BJP of controlling the ECI.

articles.headline: Youth voters comprise 20% of Tamil Nadu electorate for upcoming polls
articles.summary: Young voters aged 18-29 make up 20.7% of Tamil Nadu's 5.67 crore electors, positioning them as a decisive group in the April Assembly elections. This includes 12.51 lakh first-time voters. Political analysts suggest this demographic may favor new political entrants promising structural change. The Election Commission is focusing on maximizing participation among youngsters.

articles.headline: Election Commission announces polling for five states from April 9
articles.summary: The Election Commission announced polling for Assam, Kerala, Puducherry, Tamil Nadu, and West Bengal starting April 9. West Bengal will vote in two phases, while Assam will have a single-phase election. Counting for all 824 constituencies is scheduled for May 4. Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar stated that 174 million electors are eligible to vote.

articles.headline: West Bengal parties compete for youth vote with welfare schemes
articles.summary: With Assembly elections approaching, West Bengal's political parties are targeting the decisive youth demographic. The TMC government launched the Banglar Yuba-Sathi Scheme, offering Rs 1,500 monthly to unemployed youth. The BJP has countered by focusing on job creation and startups. Young voters (21-44) previously showed a high turnout of over 83% in the state.

articles.headline: Maoist insurgency in India enters decline ahead of March deadline
articles.summary: Mass surrenders of Maoist cadres in Telangana and Chhattisgarh suggest the long-running insurgency may be nearing its end. LWE incidents declined 81% to 374 in 2024. The government has set a March 31 deadline to eliminate Left-Wing Extremism. Increased infrastructure spending and targeted rehabilitation policies offering up to Rs 5 lakh are credited for the weakening of the 'Red Corridor.'

articles.headline: Centre revokes Sonam Wangchuk’s detention to facilitate Ladakh dialogue
articles.summary: Activist Sonam Wangchuk was released from Jodhpur Central Jail after the Centre revoked his detention under the National Security Act. Wangchuk had been held for six months following violent protests for Ladakh statehood and Sixth Schedule inclusion. The government stated the revocation is intended to facilitate a 'meaningful' dialogue with agitating Ladakh groups.

articles.headline: Karnataka Assembly session begins amid political uncertainty
articles.summary: The Karnataka Legislative Assembly began its session on Tuesday amid political uncertainty following recent elections. The new government faces challenges in forming its cabinet.

articles.headline: Congress forms committee to strategize for upcoming elections
articles.summary: The Indian National Congress on Tuesday formed a committee to devise strategies for upcoming state and national elections. The party aims to strengthen its organizational base and voter outreach.

articles.headline: BJP intensifies campaign for upcoming by-elections
articles.summary: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has intensified its campaign for the upcoming by-elections in three states, holding rallies and public meetings to garner support. The results will be seen as a test for the party.

articles.headline: Telangana Assembly begins monsoon session
articles.summary: The Telangana Legislative Assembly began its monsoon session on Tuesday, with the government set to present its budget and discuss key legislative proposals. The session is expected to focus on developmental issues.

articles.headline: Maharashtra government to launch new welfare scheme
articles.summary: The Maharashtra government announced a new welfare scheme on Tuesday aimed at providing financial assistance to farmers affected by crop damage due to unseasonal rains. The scheme will offer compensation and support.

articles.headline: Northeast states review development projects
articles.summary: Chief Ministers of Northeast states met on Tuesday to review the progress of key development projects in the region, focusing on infrastructure, connectivity, and tourism promotion. The meeting aimed to enhance regional cooperation.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh Assembly passes budget
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh Assembly on Tuesday passed the state budget for the fiscal year 2023-24, with significant allocations for agriculture, education, and healthcare. The budget aims to boost development.

articles.headline: Parliament deadlock continues over new legislation
articles.summary: A deadlock continued in Parliament on Tuesday over the passage of a controversial new legislation, with opposition parties demanding further debate and amendments. The government aims for swift passage.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu CM chairs meeting on drought relief
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M. K. Stalin chaired a meeting on Tuesday to assess the drought situation and discuss relief measures for farmers. The state is facing water scarcity due to poor rainfall.

articles.headline: Electoral Commission announces schedule for state elections
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India announced the schedule for upcoming state assembly elections on Tuesday, with polling to be held in three states next month. The results are anticipated to have political implications.

articles.headline: Congress criticizes government's economic policies
articles.summary: The Congress party on Tuesday criticized the government's economic policies, citing rising unemployment and inflation. The party demanded specific measures to address these issues.

articles.headline: Political parties gear up for upcoming state elections
articles.summary: Major political parties have intensified their campaigning for the upcoming state assembly elections, holding rallies and roadshows to connect with voters. The elections are seen as a litmus test for national parties.

articles.headline: Parliamentary debate on foreign policy
articles.summary: A parliamentary debate on India's foreign policy was held on Tuesday, discussing strategic partnerships, regional security, and global challenges. Lawmakers shared diverse perspectives on India's role in the world.

articles.headline: State government announces measures to curb inflation
articles.summary: A state government announced a package of measures on Tuesday to curb inflation and provide relief to consumers, including price controls on essential commodities and subsidies for food items.

articles.headline: Parliament session concludes with key legislation passed
articles.summary: The parliamentary session concluded on Tuesday with the passage of several key legislative measures, including a bill on financial services and amendments to existing laws. The government achieved its legislative agenda.

articles.headline: State government implements new environmental policy
articles.summary: A state government implemented a new environmental policy on Tuesday aimed at reducing industrial pollution and promoting sustainable development. The policy includes stricter emission norms and waste management protocols.

articles.headline: PM Modi addresses nation on Mann Ki Baat
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi addressed the nation on Tuesday through his monthly radio program 'Mann Ki Baat', highlighting achievements in various sectors and urging citizens to contribute to national progress.

articles.headline: Electoral reform proposals discussed in Parliament
articles.summary: Parliamentary discussions were held on Tuesday regarding electoral reforms, including proposals for simultaneous elections and funding transparency. Lawmakers debated various measures to improve the electoral process.

articles.headline: PM Modi inaugurates new Parliament building
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the new Parliament building on Tuesday, a landmark event marking a significant milestone in India's parliamentary history. The new structure boasts modern architecture and facilities.

articles.headline: State election results declared
articles.summary: Election results for three state assemblies were declared on Tuesday, with significant shifts in political power observed. The outcomes are expected to influence national politics.

articles.headline: West Bengal CM reviews flood relief efforts
articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee reviewed flood relief efforts on Tuesday in districts affected by heavy rainfall and waterlogging. The government is providing assistance to affected communities.

articles.headline: Parliamentary committee reviews social welfare schemes
articles.summary: A parliamentary committee reviewed the implementation of social welfare schemes on Tuesday, assessing their reach and effectiveness. The committee recommended measures to improve delivery and coverage.

articles.headline: Parliament debates national security policy
articles.summary: A parliamentary debate on national security policy was held on Tuesday, discussing defence preparedness, border security, and counter-terrorism strategies. Lawmakers emphasized strengthening national defence capabilities.

articles.headline: State government announces relief package for farmers
articles.summary: A state government announced a relief package on Tuesday for farmers affected by crop damage due to unseasonal rains and pest infestation. The package includes financial aid and input support.

articles.headline: State government announces new economic stimulus package
articles.summary: A state government announced a new economic stimulus package on Tuesday aimed at boosting growth and creating jobs. The package includes incentives for businesses and support for employment generation.

articles.headline: Assembly Polls Begin April 9; West Bengal Votes in Two Phases
articles.summary: Assembly elections in Assam, Puducherry, Kerala (April 9), Tamil Nadu (April 23), and West Bengal (April 23 & 29) announced by EC. Counting for all on May 4. Model Code of Conduct in effect. Byelections for eight Assembly seats also on April 9/23. 17.4 crore electors to vote in 824 constituencies. West Bengal will have 152 seats in phase 1, 142 in phase 2.

articles.headline: BJP Accused of Poaching Odisha Congress MLAs in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar alleged BJP offered ₹5 crore to Odisha Congress MLAs to cross-vote in Rajya Sabha polls. Two accused, Birendra Prasad and Byatarayanapura Suresh, were caught at Wonderla Resort, Bengaluru. Blank cheques recovered. FIR yet to be registered.

articles.headline: Bengaluru Civic Polls in May, DKS Urges Party Unity
articles.summary: Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar announced Bengaluru civic polls in May. He urged Congress aspirants to work for the party regardless of ticket, fixing voter list discrepancies. Over 1,500 aspirants applied for tickets, which will be announced after poll dates are declared.

articles.headline: Karnataka Byelections for Davangere South, Bagalkot on April 9
articles.summary: Byelections for Davangere South and Bagalkot Assembly seats on April 9, necessitated by deaths of Shamanur Shivashankarappa and H.Y. Meti. Counting on May 4. Congress aims to retain, BJP expects victory. Intense internal fights over candidate selection in both parties, especially Davangere South, where Muslim leaders seek a Muslim candidate.

articles.headline: BJP Slams State Govt Over Unanswered Questions in Assembly
articles.summary: Opposition BJP criticized the Karnataka government, asking if officials were "playing golf" instead of assisting Ministers with Assembly questions. Leader R. Ashok raised the issue after many unstarred questions went unanswered, impacting House functioning. Deputy Speaker was directed to examine the matter.

articles.headline: Billava/Ediga MLAs Upset Over Meagre Funds for Narayana Guru Corporation
articles.summary: MLAs from the Billava/Ediga community expressed dissatisfaction with ₹10 crore allocation to Sri Narayana Guru Development Corporation. They demanded at least ₹100 crore. Minister Shivaraj Tangadagi defended the allocation, stating the Congress government upgraded a BJP-created cell, and promised additional funds.

articles.headline: Chariot Decoration Row Stirs Holenarasipur Political Tensions
articles.summary: A political dispute arose in Holenarasipur over who would offer the ceremonial cloth for the Sri Lakshmi Narasimha Rathotsava chariot. Supporters of H.D. Revanna and Shreyas Patel clashed, leading district administration to decide on flower-only decoration, ending the controversy.

articles.headline: Govt. Unable to Tell Allies Actions Impact India: Khurshid
articles.summary: Former External Affairs Minister Salman Khurshid stated that the Indian government is unable to tell its allies that their actions in ongoing global conflicts (West Asia, Russia-Ukraine) are hurting India. He noted a lack of consultation between government and Opposition on foreign policy, unlike previous practice.

articles.headline: LDF Announces Kerala Poll Candidates, UDF Scrambles
articles.summary: Kerala's LDF (CPI(M) & CPI) announced 106 Assembly election candidates on April 9, immediately after EC dates. Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan is among the 81 CPI(M) candidates. The Congress-led UDF scrambles to finalize seat-sharing, facing demands for more seats from IUML and internal leadership issues.

articles.headline: RSS Backs Centre's West Asia Stance Amid Criticism
articles.summary: RSS general secretary Dattatreya Hosabale backed the Centre's handling of the West Asia conflict, saying India acts in its "supreme interest." This comes amid Congress criticism over soaring oil prices and potential fuel shortages. He added that expressing grief over Iran's Supreme Leader's death is "not wrong."

articles.headline: Congress Slams Centre Over Chabahar Port Disengagement
articles.summary: Congress's Jairam Ramesh criticized the Centre for apparent disengagement from the Chabahar Port project, calling it a "strategic setback" after no Budget allocation. He recounted India's long engagement and UPA's initial investment, accusing Modi government of repackaging it without acknowledging prior efforts.

articles.headline: NC Seeks Release of Kashmiri Youth Booked Under UAPA
articles.summary: The National Conference (NC) urged J&K LG Manoj Sinha to release Kashmiri youth booked under UAPA for participating in rallies protesting Iran’s Supreme Leader's killing. NC legislators called the FIRs and UAPA invocation a "deeply concerning precedent" and demanded withdrawal before Id.

articles.headline: West Bengal Parties Welcome Two-Phase Assembly Election
articles.summary: West Bengal's major political parties, including Trinamool Congress and BJP, welcomed the two-phase Assembly polls (April 23, 29), a departure from 2021's eight phases. CPI(M) expressed concern over 60 lakh electors under judicial adjudication potentially losing voting rights due to voter list issues.

articles.headline: Congress Slams EC, Calls for CEC Removal to Dispel Doubts
articles.summary: Congress MP Derek O’Brien slammed the Election Commission, calling its Model Code of Conduct "Modi’s Code of Campaigning." He stated that if the Opposition's notice to remove CEC Gyanesh Kumar for "partisan conduct" isn't taken up, it implies a "tacit understanding" with the Executive.

articles.headline: Shah: Congress Made Assam 'Land of Infiltrators,' BJP to Free It
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah accused Congress of "legalising" infiltration in Assam, promising BJP would "drive out each infiltrator." He said CM Himanta Biswa Sarma freed 1.51 lakh bighas from encroachers and criticized Rahul Gandhi for opposing electoral roll revision.

articles.headline: Pure Electoral Rolls Bedrock of Democracy, Says CEC Gyanesh Kumar
articles.summary: Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar hailed the special intensive revision (SIR) of electoral rolls as a "massive democratic exercise," essential for "pure electoral rolls." He thanked officials involved and emphasized violence-free, inducement-free elections. This statement came amid an Opposition motion seeking his removal.

articles.headline: Modi Lauds Speaker Birla for 'Impartial' Leadership
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi commended Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla for his "dedication, patience, and impartiality" in conducting House proceedings. Modi congratulated MPs for rejecting the no-confidence motion against Birla, stating his actions strengthen parliamentary democracy. Birla's leadership was tested by political disagreement turning into disrespect.

articles.headline: 'Pandit' Question in U.P. Police Exam Sparks Row
articles.summary: A question in a Uttar Pradesh police recruitment exam asking for a term for "a person who changes according to circumstances" with "Pandit" as an option, sparked an uproar. Political parties and Brahmin groups alleged it was derogatory. CM Yogi Adityanath ordered blacklisting of paper setters and probes.

articles.headline: Rajasthan Property Bill Draws Scrutiny Over 'Disturbed Areas'
articles.summary: Rajasthan's new Bill regulates property transfers in "disturbed areas," requiring government approval for sales. Inspired by Gujarat's Act, it aims to prevent distress sales but faces concerns over potential arbitrary classification, communal bias, and reinforcement of residential segregation. Critics fear increased bureaucracy and misuse.

articles.headline: Poaching Fears, Resort Politics Mark RS Poll Eve
articles.summary: With Rajya Sabha polls on Monday, political activity intensified across states, marked by "poaching fears" and MLAs shifted to resorts. BJP is working to secure its third candidate's victory in Bihar despite short numbers. Congress also moved MLAs in Haryana and Odisha to prevent cross-voting, highlighting fierce competition.

articles.headline: Phases Shrink for Polls to 5 Assemblies
articles.summary: Assembly polls for 824 seats in Kerala, Assam, Puducherry, Tamil Nadu, and West Bengal will begin April 9, with results on May 4. The overall election span is reduced to 26 days. West Bengal will vote in two phases, a sharp decrease from previous eight phases, influenced by security force availability.

articles.headline: Crucial Test for INDIA Bloc Amid BJP’s Expansionist Plans
articles.summary: These elections are a key test for the INDIA bloc, with allies battling each other in West Bengal (TMC vs. Congress/Left) and Kerala (LDF vs. UDF). In Tamil Nadu, DMK faces an incumbency test. The outcome will influence the bloc's internal dynamics and the standing of leaders like Rahul Gandhi, Mamata Banerjee, and MK Stalin.

articles.headline: Express Grief, But Don’t Disturb Social Harmony: RSS
articles.summary: RSS general secretary Dattatreya Hosabale stated that expressing grief is natural, but such reactions must be peaceful and not disturb social harmony or target any community. This was in response to questions about marches held over the killing of Iran’s Ali Hosseini Khamenei.

articles.headline: US to Receive $10bn Fee from TikTok Sale
articles.summary: The US is expected to receive around $10 billion as a fee from investors for facilitating ByteDance's sale of TikTok's American operations to a consortium of US investors. Former President Trump described this as a "tremendous fee-plus" for the deal.

articles.headline: First batch of Indians returns from war-torn Iran
articles.summary: Around 70 Indian students and pilgrims, mostly from Jammu and Kashmir, landed in Delhi on Sunday. They escaped Iran via a four-day journey through Armenia and Dubai after Iranian airspace closed due to the conflict that began on February 28. More than 1,000 Indian students are still believed to be stranded in Iran as the Ministry of External Affairs coordinates further evacuations.

articles.headline: Security forces foil Uri infiltration bid, kill terrorist
articles.summary: Indian Army and Jammu & Kashmir Police foiled an infiltration attempt along the Line of Control in the Uri sector. Following intelligence inputs, a joint operation was launched on Saturday night, resulting in the elimination of a Pakistani terrorist. Warlike stores, including an AK rifle and ammunition, were recovered from the scene. Search operations continue in the thicket area.

articles.headline: Three trainee police sub-inspectors killed in road accident
articles.summary: Three probationary reserve sub-inspectors died and two were seriously injured after their car collided with a lorry in Chitradurga. The victims were traveling from Bengaluru to Ballari for a colleague's wedding when their vehicle was dragged beneath a heavy truck. Police have taken the lorry driver into custody and registered a case of rash and negligent driving.

articles.headline: Former gram panchayat president arrested for woman's murder
articles.summary: Mutturaj, a former Ujjini gram panchayat president, was arrested for allegedly killing a 74-year-old woman in Tumakuru. Police say he murdered the victim, who lived alone, because he was unable to repay a debt. He reportedly attempted to stage the crime as a robbery by sprinkling chilli powder and leaving liquor bottles behind. Stolen gold jewellery worth ₹18 lakh was recovered from his farm.

articles.headline: Three killed as truck hits autorickshaw in Telangana
articles.summary: A bricks-laden truck suffered a tyre burst and collided with a stationary autorickshaw in Nalgonda, killing three people and injuring six others. The accident occurred on Sunday afternoon when the driver lost control of the vehicle. One victim died on the spot, and two others succumbed to injuries at the hospital. The injured are currently undergoing treatment.

articles.headline: Four of family die of suffocation in Punganuru
articles.summary: Three children and an elderly man died of suffocation in Punganuru after a motorcycle engine was left running inside their home overnight. The homeowner was reportedly advised by a mechanic to keep the engine on to assist repairs. Fumes from the vehicle filled the room where the family was sleeping. Police have registered a case under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita.

articles.headline: Four injured in LPG cylinder blast in Jharkhand
articles.summary: An explosion involving 50-60 illegally stored LPG cylinders left four people critically injured in Jharkhand's Palamu district. The blast triggered a fire on the ground floor of a building at Bairiya Chowk on Sunday. Police are investigating the cause of the fire and the legality of the massive storage of gas cylinders in a residential area.

articles.headline: Two children killed in suspected LPG blast in Chennai
articles.summary: A suspected LPG cylinder explosion in Chennai's Navallur killed two children and injured seven others on Sunday morning. The blast damaged several nearby houses in Chamundeeswarar Nagar. Authorities are assessing the extent of the damage as the injured victims receive treatment for burn injuries at a local hospital.

articles.headline: Engine of crashed air ambulance found in Jharkhand
articles.summary: Villagers in Jharkhand's Chatra district discovered the engine of a crashed air ambulance deep in the Kasiyatu-Karamtand forest. The find occurred 21 days after the primary wreckage was located nearly a kilometre away. Officials expect the recovered engine to provide critical technical evidence for the ongoing investigation into the February 23 disaster.

articles.headline: Labour Commissioner directs Samsung and union to attend conciliation talks
articles.summary: The Office of the Commissioner of Labour has called for a conciliation meeting between Samsung India Electronics and the workers union on March 16. The union issued a strike notice demanding the revocation of 27 worker terminations at the Sriperumbudur factory. The talks aim to resolve the ongoing dispute following the February 13 strike notice regarding labor rights.

articles.headline: Over 70 Indian citizens return from conflict-hit Iran via Armenia
articles.summary: The first batch of more than 70 Indians, mostly students from Jammu and Kashmir, arrived in New Delhi after being stranded in Iran. The evacuation was coordinated by the Ministry of External Affairs through a land-and-air route involving Armenia and Dubai. Arrangements continue to facilitate the return of remaining Indian nationals from the conflict zone in the coming days.

articles.headline: Rural women banking agents facilitate ₹31,000 crore in annual transactions
articles.summary: Over 13.5 lakh 'bank mitras' are driving financial inclusion in rural India, with women agents in Uttar Pradesh alone facilitating transactions worth ₹31,000 crore. The government's 'Lakhpati Didi' initiative aims to turn 4 crore women into sustainable income earners. Strengthening the ecosystem for female banking correspondents is critical for converting access into economic agency.

articles.headline: ICAR to launch national gender platform for women in farming
articles.summary: The Indian Council of Agricultural Research is developing a national platform connecting over 900 institutions to boost women's roles in agri-food systems. Director General ML Jat announced the initiative following an international conference. The platform will link ICAR institutes and universities to strengthen research and capacity-building focused specifically on women farmers.

articles.headline: Monsoon expected to hit Kerala by June 4
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) on Tuesday forecast that the southwest monsoon is expected to arrive in Kerala by June 4. The monsoon is crucial for the country's agriculture and water security.

articles.headline: Bengaluru police bust inter-state drug racket
articles.summary: Bengaluru police on Tuesday arrested seven people and seized drugs worth Rs 5 crore in a major breakthrough against an inter-state drug racket. The investigation is ongoing.

articles.headline: Odisha CM reviews post-cyclone restoration efforts
articles.summary: Odisha Chief Minister Naveen Patnaik reviewed the progress of post-cyclone restoration and rehabilitation efforts on Tuesday in the affected coastal districts. The government is focused on providing aid and rebuilding infrastructure.

articles.headline: Centre forms task force for disaster management
articles.summary: The central government has formed a high-level task force on Tuesday to review and strengthen disaster management strategies and response mechanisms across the country. The focus is on preparedness and mitigation.

articles.headline: Manipur government extends internet ban
articles.summary: The Manipur government has extended the ban on internet services in the state for another five days, citing concerns over the spread of misinformation and potential for further violence. The ban has been in place since early May.

articles.headline: India's agricultural output projected to rise
articles.summary: India's agricultural output is projected to rise by 2.5% in the current fiscal year, driven by favorable monsoon forecasts and increased acreage under cultivation. This will support food security.

articles.headline: India's population growth rate stabilizes
articles.summary: India's population growth rate has stabilized, with the total fertility rate declining to 2.0 children per woman. This demographic shift has implications for economic and social development.

articles.headline: Three Bengaluru Policemen Die in Road Accident
articles.summary: Three reserve sub-inspectors from Bengaluru, Manjunath, Amaresh, and Sachin, died, and two others were injured when their car rammed a lorry near Challakere, Chitradurga. They were traveling to Ballari for a friend's wedding. Challakere police registered a case.

articles.headline: DNT Leaders Seek Separate Census Count for Communities
articles.summary: Denotified, Nomadic, and Semi-nomadic Tribe (DNT) communities approached the Supreme Court, seeking a distinct enumeration in the 2027 Census with a specific question. They argue the absence of this data has prevented members from accessing government benefits and sought specific directions to the Registrar General.

articles.headline: Pakistani Terrorist Killed Near LoC in Baramulla
articles.summary: An unidentified Pakistani terrorist was killed near the Line of Control (LoC) in Baramulla district. The Army launched a joint operation following a specific infiltration warning, engaging the terrorist who opened indiscriminate fire. An AK rifle, pistols, and large quantities of ammunition were recovered.

articles.headline: Indian oil tanker survives drone attack in UAE
articles.summary: The Indian-flagged oil tanker Jag Laadki was unharmed during a drone strike on Fujairah port in the UAE. Carrying 80,800 metric tonnes of crude oil, the vessel has set sail for India. The government confirmed that all Indian seafarers in the region are currently safe. The incident follows Iranian threats against major UAE ports used as logistics hubs by US and Israeli forces.

articles.headline: Second oil tanker reaches Mumbai via Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The Liberian-flagged vessel Smyrni, carrying 135,000 metric tonnes of crude oil for HPCL, reached Mumbai on Sunday. It is the second India-bound ship to navigate the strategic Strait of Hormuz since the conflict erupted. Currently, 22 Indian-flagged vessels with 611 seafarers remain west of the strait, and the government is monitoring their safety through the Directorate General of Shipping.

articles.headline: BAMUL president complains to CCI over Flipkart milk prices
articles.summary: Bangalore Milk Union Ltd president DK Suresh filed a complaint with the Competition Commission of India against Flipkart for selling milk for ₹1. Suresh alleged 'predatory pricing' through investor-funded discounts, claiming it hurts farmer livelihoods and dairy cooperatives. Flipkart defended the practice as a periodic promotional campaign that does not impact procurement prices paid to sellers.

articles.headline: Investors seek cover as Nifty faces 52-week low
articles.summary: Investors are ramping up portfolio hedges as the Nifty 50 faces a potential slump to its 52-week low of 21,743. The benchmark has dropped 8% since the war began. Demand for put options has surged 15 times in two weeks as high oil prices spook markets. FPIs have been the largest buyers of protective contracts while analysts warn of a structural downtrend.

articles.headline: Gen Z to drive $1.3 trillion spending by 2030
articles.summary: A report by Redseer Strategy Consultants projects that Gen Z will comprise 27% of India’s population and command $1.3 trillion in consumption by 2030. This demographic prioritizes aesthetics, sustainability, and authenticity over brand labels. One in two Gen Z women reportedly allocates over 20% of disposable income to beauty and personal care products.

articles.headline: Indian carmakers continue EV push despite global slowdown
articles.summary: While global manufacturers report $60 billion in EV write-downs, Indian carmakers like Maruti Suzuki, Tata Motors, and Mahindra remain bullish. Industry experts expect EV penetration to reach 15-20% in the next four years, supported by government incentives including the ₹25,000 crore PLI scheme and low GST rates compared to conventional vehicles.

articles.headline: upGrad to acquire Unacademy in 100% share swap deal
articles.summary: Edtech firm upGrad has signed a term sheet to acquire Unacademy through a 100% share swap. The valuation remains undisclosed until the transaction closes formally. Unacademy CEO Gaurav Munjal confirmed the deal on social media. The move comes as Unacademy also launches a ₹50 crore ESOP buyback programme, signaling major consolidation in the Indian edtech sector.

articles.headline: Electric vehicle discounts exceed ₹5 lakh to boost March sales
articles.summary: Indian automakers have introduced aggressive discounts exceeding ₹5 lakh on electric vehicles to boost sales before the fiscal year-end. Incentives on models like the Hyundai Ioniq 5 and Mahindra XUV400 are driven by fuel volatility and tax benefits. Businesses can claim 40% accelerated depreciation on EVs, making them attractive for corporate fleet electrification.

articles.headline: Plastics industry seeks government support amid rising polymer prices
articles.summary: The All India Plastics Manufacturers Association has requested a survival support package for MSMEs as polymer prices rose up to 59% in March. The price hikes are attributed to global uncertainties and supply disruptions from the West Asia conflict. The association warns that imminent factory closures could lead to high unemployment if prices are not stabilised.

articles.headline: Hindalco warns of supply disruptions in aluminium extrusion business
articles.summary: Hindalco Industries has notified stock exchanges of potential supply disruptions in its aluminium extrusion segment following a force majeure declaration by gas suppliers. The company stated the impact is limited to less than 0.1% of overall operations, and primary aluminium production remains normal. Hindalco operates extrusion plants in Uttar Pradesh, Kerala, and Andhra Pradesh.

articles.headline: AEPC requests 20% commercial LPG quota for Tamil Nadu apparel exporters
articles.summary: The Apparel Export Promotion Council urged the Tamil Nadu government to include the apparel industry within the 20% commercial LPG cylinder allotment during the current shortage. LPG is critical for processing segments like dyeing and finishing. AEPC noted that uninterrupted supply is essential to sustain operations and protect jobs for lakhs of employees in the export ecosystem.

articles.headline: Priority allocation of commercial LPG cylinders begins for hospitality sector
articles.summary: State governments have begun allocating commercial LPG cylinders to priority sectors including restaurants and hotels. In Delhi, restaurants are placed at the third level of priority with 42% allocation, while hotels and guest houses receive 4% at the fourth level. The move follows central directions to provide relief to the hospitality industry facing fuel supply shortages.

articles.headline: Aurobindo Pharma stock breaks key resistance after 5.3% weekly gain
articles.summary: Shares of Aurobindo Pharma appreciated 5.3% last week, breaking a key resistance level at ₹1,240. Technical analysis suggests a bullish trend, with the stock potentially reaching ₹1,600 in the near term. Traders are advised to consider a buy at ₹1,296, with a stop-loss at ₹1,160. The ₹1,240 level is now expected to act as a support for the pharmaceutical major.

articles.headline: Eastman Auto & Power commissions 800-MW solar module facility in Haryana
articles.summary: Eastman Auto & Power launched an 800-MW solar photovoltaic module manufacturing plant in Sonipat, Haryana. The facility will support residential and commercial solar adoption under schemes like PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana. The expansion aims to reduce dependence on imported solar modules and strengthen India's renewable energy supply chain for integrated systems.

articles.headline: IKEA India plans to double investment and reach profitability by 2028
articles.summary: IKEA India CEO Patrik Antoni announced plans to double investment to ₹21,000 crore by 2030. The retailer aims to quadruple sales and achieve profitability by 2028. Currently operating six stores, IKEA plans to add up to 30 more and increase local sourcing. Online business already contributes 30% of revenues as the firm adapts its product catalog to Indian living habits.

articles.headline: Indian fintechs simplify cross-border payments for MSMEs and freelancers
articles.summary: Startups like Razorpay and Skydo are building digital infrastructure to streamline cross-border payments for Indian exporters and freelancers. By bypassing traditional intermediary bank networks, these firms reduce transaction fees and automate regulatory compliance. The segment is growing rapidly, with volumes increasing 250% year-on-year for some payment aggregators.

articles.headline: Marine insurance premiums surge 400% due to West Asia maritime conflict
articles.summary: War-risk insurance premiums for marine hull and cargo have increased by 400% following attacks in the Strait of Hormuz. Reinsurers like GIC Re have restricted coverage, forcing Indian insurers to review risks on a per-voyage basis. The Indian government is considering a $1 billion maritime war risk pool to provide relief to shipping lines transiting through high-risk zones.

articles.headline: Airbus projects demand for 1,000 civil helicopters in India by 2045
articles.summary: Airbus Helicopters expects to deliver over 1,000 civil helicopters to India over the next two decades. The company has established a final assembly line for H125 helicopters in Karnataka, with first deliveries expected in 2027. Airbus is also vying for defense contracts to replace the Indian armed forces' ageing Cheetah and Chetak fleets, emphasizing local manufacturing.

articles.headline: Indian services sector hit by rising fuel and food costs
articles.summary: The West Asia crisis has trickled down to India's services sector, with airlines passing higher ATF costs to passengers. Hospitality and e-commerce firms face LPG shortages and rising food input costs, potentially raising restaurant prices by 25-30%. Logistics firms report workforce shortages as daily life for migrant workers is upended by cooking gas scarcity.

articles.headline: iPhone production value in India projected to hit $70 billion
articles.summary: iPhone production in India is set to reach $70 billion in freight-on-board value cumulatively between FY22 and FY26. Exports to the United States have surged, partially due to a 20% fentanyl tax imposed by the US on Chinese goods. Vendors Foxconn and Tata Electronics expect FY26 production to exceed $25 billion, accounting for 36% of the five-year total under the PLI scheme.

articles.headline: NRIs contribute 32% to DLF property sales in nine months
articles.summary: Real estate major DLF reported property sales worth Rs 5,250 crore to non-resident Indians (NRIs) between April and December. This contribution represents 32.5% of the company's total sales bookings of Rs 16,176 crore. MD Aakash Ohri noted a significant surge in NRI interest, increasing from 5% in previous years to nearly 30% currently.

articles.headline: Vedanta raises Rs 2,575 crore through oversubscribed NCD issue
articles.summary: Mining major Vedanta Ltd raised Rs 2,575 crore via non-convertible debentures after strong institutional demand. The issue included a Rs 575 crore greenshoe option following oversubscription. Priced at a coupon of 8.95%, the three-year unsecured debentures received an 'AA' rating from Icra. Participating investors included ICICI Prudential, Kotak, and Aditya Birla Sun Life mutual funds.

articles.headline: Automobile manufacturers report LPG shortage at factory canteens
articles.summary: The Society of Indian Automobile Manufacturers (SIAM) has informed the Petroleum Ministry that factory canteens are facing acute LPG shortages. If the situation persists, it could disrupt manufacturing activities for a sector that contributes 7.1% to India's GDP. The industry body requested that manufacturing canteens be given top priority for LPG supply alongside hospitals.

articles.headline: Lupin targets complex generics and biosimilars for US expansion
articles.summary: Lupin is shifting its US strategy toward complex generics, biosimilars, and respiratory therapies as traditional generics face price erosion. CEO Vinita Gupta said the firm plans to launch five biosimilars globally over five years. The company currently spends 8% of revenue on R&D. In India, Lupin is focusing on chronic therapies like cardiovascular and diabetes management.

articles.headline: Air India detects travel policy misuse by 4,000 employees
articles.summary: Air India has initiated corrective actions after an internal investigation revealed large-scale discrepancies in its leisure travel policy. Over 4,000 employees allegedly misused free air tickets by listing non-relatives as nominees or selling tickets. The airline, owned by the Tata Group, is imposing penalties on erring staff as part of its ongoing transformation plan.

articles.headline: NCR caterers shift to wood and coal amid LPG crunch
articles.summary: Caterers in Delhi-NCR are scrambling for alternative fuels as commercial LPG cylinders become scarce and expensive due to West Asia supply disruptions. Prices for wood and coal have surged by 50% as wedding orders face cancellations. Some caterers report paying up to Rs 5,000 for cylinders that previously cost Rs 1,800 to ensure events proceed.

articles.headline: Adani Total Gas reduces prices for specific industrial customers
articles.summary: Adani Total Gas Ltd cut prices for excess natural gas supplied to certain industrial users to Rs 82.95 per SCM, down from Rs 119.90. The revision reflects softening upstream prices despite ongoing supply disruptions. The company had previously asked commercial and industrial customers to curtail consumption to 40% of contracted volumes due to the halt in ships through Hormuz.

articles.headline: Jeff Bezos pushes for major strategy shift at Washington Post
articles.summary: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos is reportedly demanding higher productivity and cost-cutting at The Washington Post to stem $100 million in annual losses. Strategy changes include reducing the newsroom budget and leveraging data and AI. The newspaper recently laid off 350 journalists. Bezos aims for the publication to pay for itself rather than relying on his personal fortune.

articles.headline: Auto sector earnings at risk from high costs and war
articles.summary: The Nifty auto index has fallen 14% this month as investors worry about supply chain disruptions and rising input costs from the Iran war. Exposure to West Asia ranges from 10% to 40% for major firms like Ashok Leyland and Bajaj Auto. Analysts warn that production could be impacted if LNG supplies fall short or if freight rates continue to rise due to regional uncertainty.

articles.headline: Industry seeks duty concessions for SEZ goods sold in domestic markets
articles.summary: The Export Promotion Council for EOUs and SEZs has requested the government to allow goods from special economic zones to be sold in the domestic market at concessional duties. Currently, these units pay full Customs duties, while imports from FTA partners like China enter at zero or lower rates. The move aims to help SEZ units utilize idle capacity and ensure a level playing field.

articles.headline: India attracts real estate capital amid Dubai geopolitical risk
articles.summary: The West Asia conflict is prompting investors to shift capital from Dubai to the Indian real estate market. NRIs and family offices are seeking geographic diversification as Dubai's tax-efficient market faces regional risk. Stability in India's macro economy and regulatory reforms like Rera have made cities like Mumbai, Bengaluru, and Hyderabad attractive long-term alternatives.

articles.headline: New Delhi and Moscow discuss trade ties
articles.summary: India and Russia held talks on Tuesday to strengthen trade and economic cooperation, with a focus on increasing bilateral trade to $30 billion by 2025. Russia is a major supplier of oil and defense equipment to India.

articles.headline: SEBI proposes stricter norms for investment advisors
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) on Tuesday proposed stricter regulations for investment advisors to protect investors. The new norms aim to enhance transparency and accountability.

articles.headline: Government to sell 5% stake in Coal India
articles.summary: The government plans to sell a 5% stake in Coal India Limited through an offer-for-sale (OFS) to raise an estimated Rs 15,000 crore. The divestment is part of the government's disinvestment drive.

articles.headline: NCLT approves merger of two banks
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) has approved the merger of two public sector banks, paving the way for consolidation in the banking sector. The merger aims to improve operational efficiency.

articles.headline: India's services PMI expands to 60.1 in May
articles.summary: India's services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) expanded to 60.1 in May, indicating robust growth in the services sector. New business and output levels saw significant increases.

articles.headline: Government mulls measures to boost manufacturing sector
articles.summary: The Indian government is considering new policy measures to boost the manufacturing sector, including incentives for domestic production and export promotion. The goal is to enhance India's manufacturing capabilities.

articles.headline: Adani Group announces expansion plans
articles.summary: The Adani Group announced ambitious expansion plans on Tuesday for its infrastructure and energy businesses, including investments in green hydrogen and renewable energy projects. The group aims to achieve carbon neutrality by 2030.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank merges with HDFC Ltd.
articles.summary: HDFC Bank announced the completion of its merger with HDFC Ltd. on Tuesday, creating India's largest mortgage lender and second-largest bank by assets. The merger aims to leverage synergies.

articles.headline: India seeks enhanced trade ties with ASEAN
articles.summary: India is seeking to deepen trade and investment ties with the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) countries, with a focus on boosting regional economic integration and supply chain resilience.

articles.headline: India's e-commerce market poised for growth
articles.summary: India's e-commerce market is poised for significant growth in the coming years, driven by increasing internet penetration, smartphone usage, and changing consumer preferences. Online retail sales are expected to double.

articles.headline: India's domestic air passenger traffic grows
articles.summary: India's domestic air passenger traffic grew by 12.5% year-on-year in April, reaching a record high of 12.5 million passengers. The aviation sector is recovering strongly post-pandemic.

articles.headline: Manufacturing sector output sees steady growth
articles.summary: India's manufacturing sector output witnessed steady growth in May, supported by strong domestic demand and increased production. The PMI data reflects positive momentum in the sector.

articles.headline: Indian companies show strong earnings growth
articles.summary: Indian companies listed on stock exchanges reported strong earnings growth in the last quarter, driven by robust demand and improved operational efficiency. The corporate performance reflects economic recovery.

articles.headline: Stock markets hit new highs
articles.summary: Indian stock markets reached new all-time highs on Tuesday, driven by positive global cues and strong corporate earnings. Investor sentiment remains buoyant.

articles.headline: Services sector shows robust growth
articles.summary: India's services sector continues to show robust growth, contributing significantly to the country's GDP. Key sub-sectors like IT, finance, and hospitality are driving this expansion.

articles.headline: Indian automakers face supply chain disruptions
articles.summary: Indian automakers are facing supply chain disruptions due to global shortages of key components, impacting production schedules and vehicle availability. The industry is seeking alternative sourcing solutions.

articles.headline: Startup funding shows slight decline
articles.summary: India's startup funding saw a slight decline in the second quarter of 2023 compared to the first, attributed to global economic uncertainties. However, investments in promising startups remain strong.

articles.headline: Indian banking sector remains robust
articles.summary: India's banking sector remains robust, with improved asset quality and capital adequacy ratios. Public sector banks are showing enhanced performance and profitability.

articles.headline: Corporate earnings surpass expectations
articles.summary: Indian corporate earnings have surpassed expectations in the recent quarter, indicating a healthy corporate sector and a positive economic outlook. Many companies reported record profits.

articles.headline: Sensex and Nifty hit record highs
articles.summary: India's benchmark stock indices, Sensex and Nifty, hit new record highs on Tuesday, driven by strong investor sentiment and positive economic indicators. The market performance reflects confidence in the economy.

articles.headline: Industrial production shows mixed trends
articles.summary: India's industrial production showed mixed trends in April, with manufacturing output rising while mining and electricity sectors experienced slight slowdowns. Overall industrial growth remained positive.

articles.headline: IT sector growth remains strong
articles.summary: India's Information Technology (IT) sector continues to exhibit strong growth, driven by increasing demand for digital services and global outsourcing. The sector is a major contributor to exports.

articles.headline: Company announces new investment in infrastructure
articles.summary: A leading Indian company announced significant new investment on Tuesday in infrastructure development, focusing on transportation and energy projects. The investment aims to boost economic growth.

articles.headline: Indian startups secure significant funding
articles.summary: Indian startups secured significant funding in the last week, with investments flowing into sectors like fintech, edtech, and healthtech. The funding rounds indicate continued investor confidence.

articles.headline: New vehicle scrappage policy announced
articles.summary: The government announced a new vehicle scrappage policy on Tuesday aimed at promoting the phasing out of old and polluting vehicles and encouraging the adoption of cleaner transport. The policy includes incentives for owners.

articles.headline: Manufacturing output shows resilience
articles.summary: India's manufacturing output showed resilience in May, despite global economic headwinds. Domestic demand and government support measures contributed to sustained growth.

articles.headline: Indian IT companies expand global presence
articles.summary: Indian IT companies are expanding their global presence through acquisitions and strategic partnerships, aiming to tap into new markets and enhance service offerings.

articles.headline: IT sector sees sustained demand
articles.summary: India's IT sector continues to see sustained demand from global clients, driving growth and creating employment opportunities. The sector is adapting to new technologies and market trends.

articles.headline: Services sector growth moderates slightly
articles.summary: India's services sector growth moderated slightly in May compared to the previous month, although it remained in expansionary territory. Demand in certain sub-sectors saw a minor slowdown.

articles.headline: Indian stock markets witness volatility
articles.summary: Indian stock markets witnessed volatility on Tuesday, influenced by global economic uncertainties and domestic factors. Investors remained cautious amidst mixed market signals.

articles.headline: Prices of Cars, TVs, ACs to Rise 5-6% from April
articles.summary: Prices of consumer goods like cars, TVs, two-wheelers, refrigerators, and ACs are expected to rise 5-6% from April due to surging input costs, including crude derivatives, higher freight rates, and rupee depreciation. Automakers and electronics manufacturers are considering hikes, while some are pausing increases in March to meet sales targets.

articles.headline: European Safety Regulator Raises Alarm on Air India Lapses
articles.summary: The European Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) raised alarms about safety lapses by Air India, following inspections at European airports. Findings per inspection for Air India planes reached 1.96 in January, prompting DGCA scrutiny. The airline, facing ageing aircraft and supply chain delays for refurbishment, has seen its fault count drop to 1.76 as of last week.

articles.headline: E-Bus, Truck Makers Get Breather on Localisation Norms
articles.summary: The government has extended localization requirements for electric truck and bus makers by six months, allowing them to import traction motors using rare earth magnets until September 1. This reprieve helps companies claim incentives under the PM e-drive scheme, addressing global supply chain disruptions and China's export restrictions on heavy rare earth magnets.

articles.headline: Ready Meals, Frozen Foods Sales Surge Amid LPG Crisis
articles.summary: Sales of ready-to-cook, no-cook foods, and frozen snacks have surged up to 20% month-on-month as households conserve LPG due to the ongoing Gulf War. Companies are stocking up and promoting these products, while demand for alternate cooking aids like induction cooktops has nearly tripled.

articles.headline: Consumer Giants Shore Up Legal Leadership
articles.summary: Major consumer companies are rapidly filling leadership roles in their legal departments due to stricter regulations, new labor rules, and increased M&A activity. They seek legal leaders who are strategic partners, understand digital landscapes, and can navigate complex compliance obligations, including the new Labour Code and DPDP Act.

articles.headline: Centre in Huddle to Cushion Jet Fuel Shock for Airlines
articles.summary: India is seeking ways to shield airlines from spiraling jet fuel prices due to the West Asia conflict, aiming to prevent fare hikes and adverse effects on air travel demand. Civil aviation and petroleum ministries are negotiating with oil marketing companies to spread carriage costs over a longer timeline, preventing immediate price hikes on April 1.

articles.headline: Tapping into Bengaluru’s Diverse Talent Pool
articles.summary: Shailesh Deshpande, head of Burns & McDonnell India's Bengaluru office, discusses leveraging Bengaluru's talent pool for EPC projects and integrating AI/automation. He emphasizes a people-first culture, fostering ownership, and attracting diverse talent to support growth in Power, T&D, Global Facilities, and Environmental Services.

articles.headline: Sequoia Alumni Eye $250m Fund for Early-stage Play
articles.summary: Former Peak XV Partners MDs Shailesh Lakhani, Harshjit Sethi, and Mayank Porwal have launched Ambition Capital, an early-stage fund targeting $250 million. The fund will focus on seed and Series A investments in AI, deeptech, fintech, and India-specific solutions, adding to the growing trend of new venture capital funds in India.

articles.headline: House-help Co Snabbit Taps Mirae Asset, BII, Others for $60m
articles.summary: On-demand home help startup Snabbit is raising $50-60 million in funding led by Mirae Asset Venture Investments, with existing backer Lightspeed and new investor Susquehanna International Group also participating. The round is being finalized to support the startup's growth.

articles.headline: Travel Co Scapia Eyes $60m from General Catalyst & Others
articles.summary: Travel startup Scapia is in talks to raise $50-60 million from investors, potentially led by US-based General Catalyst. Nexus Venture Partners and existing investors may also join the round. This fundraise aims to scale Scapia’s co-branded credit card and international travel booking businesses, competing with Uni Cards and Niyo.

articles.headline: Officebanao Lands $7.7m for Workspace Interiors
articles.summary: Officebanao, a platform for workspace interiors, has raised $7.7 million in funding. The round was led by existing investor Lightspeed with participation from Mangum II and Medra Family, structured in two tranches over 2025 and 2026.

articles.headline: KC Ang Quits as Head, Tata Semicon Manufacturing
articles.summary: Chip veteran KC Ang resigned as president and head of Tata Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) in December 2025 due to personal reasons. Ang was crucial in spearheading Tata Electronics’ foundry operations. A Tata Electronics spokesperson confirmed his departure, highlighting the company's strong leadership team.

articles.headline: Most Indian GCCs Still at Early Stages of DPDP Act Compliance
articles.summary: Four months after India's DPDP Act rules were notified, most Global Capability Centres (GCCs) in India are in early compliance planning, with few having structured implementation. Experts urge treating the May 2027 deadline firmly due to extensive operational changes needed across people, processes, and technology, especially for mixed and unstructured data.

articles.headline: Fund Managers Bet on Metals, Power Amid Geopolitical Turmoil
articles.summary: Fund managers are making selective purchases in metals, energy, and power stocks amid West Asia tensions and AI-led disruption concerns. Investments include Hindalco and Nalco (integrated aluminum producers) and Siemens Energy, JSW Energy, and Power Grid (power/new energy plays), seen as beneficiaries of green energy and AI infrastructure.

articles.headline: Hedge Funds Eye Exotic Options for Cross-Asset Swings
articles.summary: Hedge funds are increasingly using exotic options like dual binaries for cross-asset hedging, responding to wild market swings, such as the $36 oil price fluctuation on March 9. These options help investors manage risk and hit target returns within strict limits amidst heightened volatility and stagflation concerns.

articles.headline: Barclays Prepares to Restart ECM Business in India
articles.summary: Barclays Plc is preparing to restart its equity capital market (ECM) business in India, ten years after discontinuing it as part of an Asia-wide purge. The move aims to secure a larger wallet share of corporate clients, complementing its existing debt and M&A advisory services, with operations expected to resume in months.

articles.headline: Flipkart Calls `1 Milk Offer a Limited Promotion After Bamul Moves CCI
articles.summary: Flipkart defended its `1 milk offer as a "limited promotional campaign" after Bamul complained to the CCI. Flipkart stated prices are seller-determined, farmers receive full agreed prices, and its promotions aim to enhance customer value. Bamul seeks a probe into deep discounting by e-commerce platforms, citing impact on cooperatives and small retailers.

articles.headline: Palantir CEO Buys Miami Mansion Before Headquarters Move
articles.summary: Palantir Technologies CEO Alex Karp bought a $46 million mansion in Miami in June last year, seven months before the company announced its headquarters move from Denver to Miami. Karp, with a $14.2 billion net worth, is among many tech moguls relocating to Florida, possibly due to tax proposals in states like California.

articles.headline: ESG Crucial for Risk Management and Long-Term Growth
articles.summary: ESG indicators are vital for identifying businesses with long-term resilience. Strong governance, environmental preparedness, and responsible practices are increasingly valued by investors and regulators (e.g., BRSR framework). ESG funds have delivered returns comparable to traditional equity, highlighting that responsible investing isn't at the cost of financial performance.

articles.headline: IEA releases oil from emergency stockpile to Asia
articles.summary: The International Energy Agency announced that oil from an unprecedented 400-million-barrel stockpile release will be made available immediately in Asia. The move aims to replace barrels lost due to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. While supplies for Asia begin flowing today, European and American markets will receive barrels from late March to help stabilize surging global energy prices.

articles.headline: Siddaramaiah urges Centre to address LPG shortage
articles.summary: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah has written to the Union government seeking urgent intervention to address cooking gas shortages in the state. Reports suggest supply disruptions are linked to West Asian tensions. The CM requested alternative options like LPG imports and noted that hoarding has led to black-marketing. Authorities have intensified inspections at bakeries and hotels to prevent domestic cylinder misuse.

articles.headline: LPG bookings dip as states crack down on hoarding
articles.summary: The Petroleum Ministry reported a decline in LPG bookings as state governments intensified raids to prevent black marketing amid West Asian supply concerns. Over 1,483 inspections were conducted in Uttar Pradesh alone, leading to multiple arrests. Despite international oil price volatility, state-run companies confirmed that refineries are maintaining adequate inventories to meet demand.

articles.headline: US-China trade talks in Paris prepare for summit
articles.summary: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng met in Paris for economic and trade talks. The meeting paves the way for a state visit by President Donald Trump to Beijing in two weeks to meet Chinese leader Xi Jinping. The summit aims to address trade tensions as the US administration prepares for a potential new logistics architecture.

articles.headline: India seeks fertilizer supplies from non-West Asian nations
articles.summary: New Delhi is increasing fertilizer imports from Morocco, Indonesia, and Russia to shield its farming sector from the West Asia conflict. Approximately 70% of India's urea imports currently pass through the Strait of Hormuz. Sourcing is being diversified to prepare for the upcoming kharif planting season and reduce strategic vulnerability to regional maritime blockades.

articles.headline: Airfares to US and Europe surge following West Asia crisis
articles.summary: Economy class airfares from India to the US and Europe have more than doubled or tripled as Gulf-based carriers suspended operations due to the West Asia crisis. Connecting flights previously costing ₹75,000 now range between ₹1.5 lakh and ₹2.45 lakh. Passengers are forced to rely on European and South-East Asian airlines, which are operating at near-full capacity.

articles.headline: West Asia crisis disrupts nearly 70% of Indian outbound shipments
articles.summary: The prolonged West Asia conflict has affected roughly 70% of Indian exports due to surging freight rates and stranded cargo. Logistics costs have increased by up to 300% as major shipping lines suspend services or take longer routes via the Cape of Good Hope. Export clusters like the Morbi ceramic industry and engineering firms face severe disruptions and gas shortages.

articles.headline: Finance Ministry revises public shareholding norms for listed companies
articles.summary: The Finance Ministry has introduced a six-slab framework for minimum public shareholding in listed companies based on post-issue capital. The revision targets mega-cap companies, allowing a graded approach to the 25% public float requirement. The timeframe to achieve this threshold is now set between three and ten years, depending on market capitalisation.

articles.headline: MSMEs seek debt moratorium as West Asia war halts gas supply
articles.summary: Small businesses in Western India are requesting a six-month moratorium on loans as gas shortages have cut production by 50-60%. The SME Chamber of India plans to meet with the RBI to discuss financial relief for metal fabrication and automotive component units struggling to complete orders by the fiscal year-end due to energy supply disruptions.

articles.headline: Centre cautious on rolling out Haryana model for fertilizer sales
articles.summary: The central government is not rushing to link fertilizer sales to actual crop requirements nationally, despite positive pilot results in Haryana. The Haryana model uses digital authentication via a state portal to curb the misuse of chemical fertilizers. Urea consumption in the state declined by 1.25 lakh tonnes, saving approximately ₹700.53 crore in subsidies.

articles.headline: Centre set to scrap IDBI Bank privatisation due to low bids
articles.summary: India's privatisation drive faced a setback as the government is likely to call off the IDBI Bank stake sale. Officials stated that bids came in below the reserved price due to global uncertainties and changing market conditions. The government and LIC had planned to sell a combined 60.7% stake in the lender. This highlights the political and structural constraints of disinvestment policy.

articles.headline: West Asia war erodes $447 billion from Indian market capitalisation
articles.summary: India’s market capitalisation fell by $447 billion since the start of the West Asia conflict, mirroring the absolute erosion seen during the March 2020 Covid-19 crash. Global market value has slumped by over $8.5 trillion. Stretched valuations and rising crude oil prices are driving the selloff, with the Nifty 50 P/E ratio falling from 19.5 to 17.8 times.

articles.headline: Economists cut India growth forecasts as West Asia crisis continues
articles.summary: ICICI Bank and Goldman Sachs have revised India’s GDP growth forecasts downward to 7% and 6.5% respectively for FY27. The war in West Asia is driving crude prices higher and disrupting trade, threatening India’s economic 'sweet spot.' High energy costs and potential supply disruptions via the Strait of Hormuz are cited as primary risks to industrial output and inflation management.

articles.headline: India faces massive energy import bill surge amid Gulf supply disruptions
articles.summary: India faces a ballooning energy import bill as global oil prices settle over $100 a barrel. The country relies on overseas supplies for 90% of its crude and 60% of its LPG. Experts estimate the net oil import bill could rise by $56-64 billion annually if Brent averages $110-115. The government has prioritised gas allocation for households over commercial users to manage shortages.

articles.headline: Gems and jewellery exports drop 20% amid West Asia conflict
articles.summary: The Gems and Jewellery Export Promotion Council (GJEPC) reported a 20% drop in exports this month due to geopolitical tensions. Executive director Sabyasachi Ray estimated that $1.2 billion in exports could be impacted over three months. While US tariffs have eased, the diversification toward UAE and Gulf markets is now being eroded by the ongoing regional instability.

articles.headline: GenZ consumption in India to hit $1.3 trillion by 2030
articles.summary: A Redseer Strategy Consultants report projects that GenZ will command $1.3 trillion in consumption by 2030, making up 27% of India's population. This generation prioritizes aesthetics and experiences, driving growth in beauty, personal care, and fashion. GenZ is expected to account for $19 billion of the BPC market and $40 billion in fitness and sports consumption by 2030.

articles.headline: Agricultural exports to West Asia at risk due to war
articles.summary: India's farm sector could see a decline in earnings as West Asia accounts for over 20% of the country's total agricultural exports. GTRI reports that $11.8 billion in exports, including rice, meat, and vegetables, face disruption. Rice exports are particularly vulnerable, with West Asia buying 36.7% of India’s global supply, critical for producers in Punjab and Haryana.

articles.headline: India secures Russian oil imports as West Asian supply crashes
articles.summary: India has moved to secure Russian oil after the US granted a one-month waiver for purchase and delivery. Russian shipments doubled to 2.1 million barrels per day (bpd) last week as West Asian supplies plummeted to 230,000 bpd. Despite the surge in Russian oil, India still faces a crude shortfall of 750,000 bpd in March compared to February due to the Hormuz blockade.

articles.headline: Rupee hits new all-time low of 92.46 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee closed at a record low of 92.46 per dollar as the West Asia conflict kept oil prices elevated. Pressure intensified after Iran threatened to block the Strait of Hormuz. The RBI has intervened in both the forex and bond markets to manage volatility, with foreign exchange reserves falling by $11.7 billion in the first week of the war.

articles.headline: House panel suggests IPOs for profitable Regional Rural Banks
articles.summary: A parliamentary committee has recommended that the government launch public offerings for highly profitable Regional Rural Banks (RRBs). This move aims to attract market capital and improve corporate governance. RRBs reported a consolidated net profit of Rs 7,720 crore in nine months, with gross NPAs falling to a 13-year low of 5.4%.

articles.headline: USTR launches Section 301 investigations into manufacturing and labor
articles.summary: The US Trade Representative initiated investigations against 16 countries, including India, regarding 'structural excess capacity' in manufacturing sectors like steel and electronics. A second probe covers 60 partners regarding goods produced with forced labor. The investigations could lead to new tariffs as the administration seeks durable mechanisms for its trade agenda.

articles.headline: Horticulture and livestock growth outpaces traditional crop farming
articles.summary: Indian agriculture is shifting toward high-value segments as horticulture, livestock, and fisheries outgrow traditional crops. Horticulture now accounts for 33% of agricultural GVA, with production exceeding food grains in 2024-25. Milk has become the largest food commodity by volume, though the sector faces challenges like fodder shortages and inadequate post-harvest infrastructure.

articles.headline: Global oil markets brace for volatile week after Kharg strike
articles.summary: Oil markets face continued instability after US strikes on Iran's Kharg Island export hub. Brent crude surged 11% last week, touching nearly $120 before closing at $103. Analysts expect crude to open higher as transit through the Strait of Hormuz remains stalled. Asian nations are reportedly seeking $50 billion in alternative energy deals with US companies to reduce reliance on the Gulf.

articles.headline: IEA to release 271 million barrels of emergency oil stocks
articles.summary: The International Energy Agency announced it will release 271.7 million barrels of oil from government stocks into Asia to replace barrels lost to war disruptions. Implementation plans from 66 member states show 72% of the release will be crude oil. The move aims to stabilize markets as regional buyers clamor for supplies amid the near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.headline: Taxpayers urged to align investments with long-term goals by March 31
articles.summary: With the financial year-end approaching, experts advise taxpayers to avoid last-minute, low-return products just for tax savings. Investors should assess suitability for PPF, ELSS, or NPS based on their chosen tax regime and risk appetite. The report emphasizes separation of insurance and investment, noting that under-insurance is a major risk when buying traditional plans in haste.

articles.headline: India's retail inflation eases to 4.25% in April
articles.summary: India's retail inflation, measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI), eased to a 15-month low of 4.25% in April, down from 4.83% in March. Food and fuel prices contributed to the decline.

articles.headline: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 6.5%
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) on Tuesday decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the eighth consecutive time. The decision was taken to support economic growth while ensuring inflation remains within the target.

articles.headline: Inflationary pressures remain a concern
articles.summary: Despite a decline in retail inflation, inflationary pressures remain a concern for the Indian economy, with the central bank closely monitoring price stability. Global commodity prices pose a risk.

articles.headline: Global economic slowdown poses challenges for India
articles.summary: The global economic slowdown and geopolitical uncertainties pose challenges for India's economic growth, according to a report released on Tuesday. The report recommends policy interventions to mitigate risks.

articles.headline: RBI tightens norms for lending to NBFCs
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) on Tuesday tightened its norms for bank lending to Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) to manage systemic risks. The move aims to ensure financial stability in the sector.

articles.headline: India's foreign exchange reserves rise
articles.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves rose by $3.2 billion to $589.2 billion in the week ended May 26, driven by an increase in foreign currency assets. The reserves provide a buffer against external shocks.

articles.headline: Fitch upgrades India's outlook to stable
articles.summary: Fitch Ratings on Tuesday upgraded India's sovereign rating outlook to stable from negative, citing strong economic growth and fiscal consolidation efforts. The upgrade reflects improved economic prospects.
