articles.summary: Daniil Medvedev defeated World No. 1 Carlos Alcaraz 6-3, 7-6(3) at Indian Wells, snapping Alcaraz's perfect 2026 streak. Medvedev will now face Jannik Sinner in the final, who beat Alexander Zverev 6-2, 6-4.

articles.headline: Rishabh Singh Century Leads North Zone to Win
articles.summary: Rishabh Singh scored an unbeaten 100 off 54 balls, his second century, to lead North Zone to a 10-wicket victory over South Zone in the J.K. Bose inter-zonal T20 title. North successfully chased South's target of 133 with ease and is set to face East on Monday.

articles.headline: Chandrika Pujari Wins Gold, India Bags Five Medals
articles.summary: India concluded its World Boxing Futures Cup campaign with five medals, including a gold by Chandrika Pujari (women’s 51kg) who defeated Uzbekistan’s Mardonova Nazokat. Gunjan (48kg), Joyshree Devi (54kg), and L. Ambekar Meetei (men’s 50kg) secured silver medals.

articles.headline: Archer Rishabh Yadav Optimistic for Asian, Olympic Games
articles.summary: Compound archer Rishabh Yadav, a 2025 World Championships medalist, is focused on the upcoming Asian Games and 2028 Olympics despite a minor slump in recent trials. He views the Asia Cup stage as crucial preparation for the Asian Games, where he anticipates stiff competition among top archers.

articles.headline: NorthEast United FC Claims First ISL Victory Over Jamshedpur
articles.summary: NorthEast United FC secured their first Indian Super League victory, defeating Jamshedpur FC 2-1. Andy Rodriguez scored both goals for the Highlanders, moving them to seventh place with six points. Mumbai City FC also won, beating Inter Kashi FC 2-1.

articles.headline: Antonelli's Maiden F1 Win: A Mercedes Triumph
articles.summary: Kimi Antonelli's dominant Chinese GP win marks the culmination of Mercedes' faith in him as Hamilton's successor, strengthening early predictions of an intra-team title battle. While Ferrari shows improvement, McLaren struggles with technical issues and Red Bull faces reliability problems.

articles.headline: FC Barcelona's Future Threatened by Two Billion-Euro Debt
articles.summary: FC Barcelona, owned by 114,000 members, faces a two billion-euro debt, the highest for any football club. President Joan Laporta attributes this to mismanagement and Covid-19, selling assets and renovating Camp Nou. Opponent Víctor Font warns of external investors destroying the club's unique model.

articles.headline: Antonelli, 19, Becomes Second-Youngest F1 Race Winner
articles.summary: Kimi Antonelli, 19, won the Chinese Grand Prix, becoming the second-youngest F1 race winner in history. The Italian pulled clear of a battle between Russell and two Ferraris to secure a 1-2 finish for Mercedes. Team principal Toto Wolff defended picking Antonelli, who joined Mercedes' junior program at age 11.

articles.headline: Spain Versus Argentina Football Match Called Off Due to Gulf Conflict
articles.summary: The "Finalissima" match between European champions Spain and Copa America winners Argentina, scheduled for March 27 in Qatar, has been cancelled. UEFA cited difficulties and logistics due to the ongoing West Asia conflict, with Argentina rejecting multiple alternative arrangements.

articles.headline: F1 Faces $132M Loss as Bahrain, Saudi GPs Cancelled Amid West Asia Turmoil
articles.summary: Formula 1 is set to lose an estimated $132 million (₤100m) after the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grands Prix were cancelled. This is due to the war between Iran, the USA, and Israel, making it unlikely for the races to be replaced in 2026. Saudi Arabia and Bahrain pay F1 an estimated $72m and $60m respectively.

articles.headline: Barcelona Thrash Sevilla 5-2 to Stay Top of LaLiga
articles.summary: FC Barcelona secured a 5-2 victory over Sevilla in LaLiga, maintaining a four-point lead at the top of the table. Raphinha scored a hat-trick, opening with a penalty and adding two more goals. Dani Olmo and Joao Cancelo also scored for Hansi Flick's side.

articles.headline: India’s energy storage market nearly doubles in one year to 224 GWh
articles.summary: India's stationary energy storage market surged from 122 GWh to 224 GWh in a single year. The project pipeline includes 60 GWh under execution and 92 GWh in BESS tenders. Major developments include Adani's world-record BESS project and upcoming long-duration storage demonstrations by NTPC. The sector is poised for a breakthrough as India commissions 5 GWh of new capacity.

articles.headline: Pahwa Group focuses on carbon capture and energy-saving air technologies
articles.summary: The Gurugram-based Pahwa Group is expanding into carbon dioxide capture and moisture-to-water technologies. With over 130 patents, the group's companies like Bry-Air provide energy-efficient dehumidifiers used globally. The firm is developing industrial systems capable of generating 1,000 litres of water per hour from air, targeting sustainable industrial and pharmaceutical sectors.

articles.headline: Researchers discover new species of butterfly in Western Ghats
articles.summary: A team of scientists has discovered a new species of butterfly, named 'Papilio indica', in the Western Ghats region of India. The discovery highlights the rich biodiversity of the area.

articles.headline: Scientists develop new method for plastic recycling
articles.summary: Indian scientists have developed a new, eco-friendly method for recycling plastic waste, potentially reducing environmental pollution. The process uses a novel catalyst to break down plastics.

articles.headline: IIT Madras develops affordable water purification system
articles.summary: Researchers at IIT Madras have developed an affordable and efficient water purification system using nanotechnology, which can provide clean drinking water in rural areas. The system is designed for low-resource settings.

articles.headline: Scientists achieve breakthrough in fusion energy research
articles.summary: Indian scientists have achieved a significant breakthrough in fusion energy research, demonstrating sustained plasma confinement. This development could pave the way for clean energy generation.

articles.headline: Scientists develop new technique for water conservation
articles.summary: Indian scientists have developed a new technique for water conservation using soil moisture sensors and smart irrigation systems, aiming to improve agricultural efficiency and reduce water wastage.

articles.headline: National Aeronautics and Space Administration launches new satellite
articles.summary: NASA launched a new satellite on Tuesday to study Earth's climate and weather patterns. The satellite will provide crucial data for climate modeling and prediction.

articles.headline: Scientists detect gravitational waves from merging black holes
articles.summary: An international team of scientists, including researchers from India, detected gravitational waves from the merger of two black holes on Tuesday. The discovery provides insights into cosmic phenomena.

articles.headline: Scientists discover novel antibiotic
articles.summary: Indian scientists have discovered a novel antibiotic compound effective against drug-resistant bacteria, offering a potential solution to the growing challenge of antimicrobial resistance. Further testing is underway.

articles.headline: Scientists develop advanced material for solar cells
articles.summary: Indian scientists have developed an advanced material that enhances the efficiency of solar cells, potentially leading to cheaper and more effective solar energy generation.

articles.headline: Scientists develop new method for carbon capture
articles.summary: Indian scientists have developed a new, efficient method for carbon capture and utilization, which could help mitigate greenhouse gas emissions and combat climate change.

articles.headline: Scientists develop new material for water purification
articles.summary: Indian scientists have developed a new, cost-effective material for water purification that removes contaminants efficiently, addressing water scarcity challenges. The material uses nanotechnology for enhanced filtration.

articles.headline: Scientists develop new material for semiconductor industry
articles.summary: Indian scientists have developed a new material that could revolutionize the semiconductor industry, enabling faster and more energy-efficient electronic devices. The material offers improved conductivity.

articles.headline: Scientists develop new sensor for environmental monitoring
articles.summary: Indian scientists have developed a new sensor for environmental monitoring that can detect pollutants in air and water with high sensitivity and accuracy. The technology aims to improve pollution control.

articles.headline: Scientists develop AI for personalized medicine
articles.summary: Indian scientists have developed an AI platform for personalized medicine, which analyzes patient data to tailor treatment plans and improve health outcomes. The technology aims to revolutionize healthcare.

articles.headline: Bangalore University to Host Science Fest Tomorrow
articles.summary: Bangalore University will organize the Bangalore University Science Festival-2026 on March 17 and 18. The event aims to provide a platform for scientific learning, knowledge exchange, and interaction among researchers, academicians, and students on the Jnana Bharathi campus.

articles.headline: Melting Glacier Ice Patches Pose Greater Flood Threat: ISRO
articles.summary: ISRO scientists' study on the Aug 5, 2025 Uttarakhand flash flood links it to the collapse of an ice patch on Srikanta Glacier, indicating ongoing deglaciation. Exposed ice patches signal high flood likelihood. The study emphasizes monitoring glaciers for early warnings beyond glacial lake outburst floods.

articles.headline: Trisulphide Metathesis Discovered for Recyclable Plastics
articles.summary: Researchers at Flinders University discovered trisulphide metathesis, a new room-temperature reaction swapping sulphur-sulphur bonds. This allows modification of fragile drugs and creation of fully recyclable plastics, offering new avenues for material science and medicine without harsh conditions.

articles.headline: Ministry of Power pilots India Energy Stack for peer-to-peer electricity trading
articles.summary: The Ministry of Power is developing the India Energy Stack (IES) to enable peer-to-peer electricity trading. Similar to UPI, IES will allow small generators to sell power directly to buyers with automated payments. The framework aims to improve grid management, regulatory compliance, and the visibility of distributed energy resources across the national distribution network.

articles.headline: IT professionals increasingly move to Global Capability Centres for stability
articles.summary: Amid a slowdown in IT services, Indian tech talent is migrating to Global Capability Centres (GCCs). Professionals are drawn to the ownership of core technology initiatives and innovation-focused roles in AI. GCCs in India now employ nearly 2 million people, offering compensation packages 12-20% higher than traditional service firms and better long-term career stability.

articles.headline: Meta workforce could shrink by 20% to fund AI infrastructure
articles.summary: Meta is planning sweeping layoffs that could affect 20% or more of its workforce. The company seeks to offset the massive costs of artificial intelligence infrastructure and prepare for greater efficiency from AI-assisted employees. While Meta spokespeople called the reporting 'speculative,' the magnitude of the cuts reflects a shift in strategy toward AI-native operations.

articles.headline: Indian startups turn AI Summit leads into global partnerships
articles.summary: A month after the India AI Impact Summit, homegrown startups report significant business outcomes including global alliances and product launches. GNANI.ai received leads from Europe and West Asia, while Skye Air launched an autonomous drone delivery service in Gurugram. The summit, which drew 600,000 visitors, highlighted India’s shift from being an AI consumer to a global provider.

articles.headline: AI rewrites military strategy as war enters decision compression era
articles.summary: Artificial Intelligence is transforming global conflict by accelerating target selection and military engagement. US military tools can now generate 1,000 potential targets daily, significantly reducing the time between identification and strikes. In India, firms like ideaForge and Torus Robotics are leading the charge in unmanned aerial and ground vehicles for border defense.

articles.headline: Deepfake attacks in India rise 900% with women most targeted
articles.summary: A pi-labs report warns of a digital deception epidemic in India, with a 900% surge in deepfake content. More than 90% of explicit deepfakes target women, primarily aged 18 to 30. Despite the surge, over one-third of women take no action due to stigma. Government data shows cybercrime complaints from women jumped 60% in two years, reaching nearly 80,000 by 2026.

articles.headline: IISc incubator to boost funding for Indian deep-tech startups
articles.summary: The Foundation for Science, Innovation and Development at IISc is working to launch a $25 million fund for deep-tech startups. The incubator supports 140 companies working on satellite tech, turbines, and diagnostics. The initiative aims to reduce India's dependence on foreign semiconductors and medical tech, helping faculty-led startups reach a stage suitable for larger VC investment.

articles.headline: Experts warn AI safeguards must match pace of LLM deployment
articles.summary: AI Safety Connect cofounders warned that the rapid diffusion of Large Language Models (LLMs) creates systemic security risks. They argued that technical capabilities are outpacing industrial stability and controls. India is uniquely positioned to lead in AI safety by investing in testing, evaluation, and validation tools, creating a potential $300 billion domestic opportunity by 2030.

articles.headline: Small Language Models crucial for India's linguistic diversity
articles.summary: The Office of the Principal Scientific Advisor suggested that India must develop Small Language Models (SLMs) alongside indigenous LLMs. SLMs are more energy-efficient and can be fine-tuned for sector-specific tasks in local languages. This strategy aims to reduce reliance on biased foreign models and ensure AI remains relevant to India’s diverse linguistic and social context.

articles.headline: Accel and Google AI fund back five Indian startups
articles.summary: The 2026 Atoms AI Cohort, backed by Accel and Google’s AI Futures Fund, has selected five Indian startups for early-stage funding. Each firm will receive up to $2 million plus cloud computing credits. The startups span sectors including scientific research, enterprise software, and voice AI. The program aims to help Indian founders compete on a global stage from day one.

articles.headline: India's tech sector sees record funding in Q1 2023
articles.summary: India's technology sector attracted record funding of $15 billion in the first quarter of 2023, driven by investments in startups and IT services. The sector continues to be a major growth driver for the economy.

articles.headline: New Delhi considers stricter regulations for AI
articles.summary: The Indian government is considering introducing stricter regulations for Artificial Intelligence (AI) to address ethical concerns and potential risks. The move comes amid growing global discussions on AI governance.

articles.headline: New Delhi hosts summit on space exploration
articles.summary: India hosted an international summit on space exploration on Tuesday, where experts discussed advancements in satellite technology and interplanetary missions. The summit aimed to foster collaboration.

articles.headline: Researchers develop AI tool for early cancer detection
articles.summary: A team of researchers has developed an AI-powered tool that can detect certain types of cancer at an early stage with high accuracy. The technology could revolutionize cancer screening.

articles.headline: IIT Bombay develops low-cost solar panel
articles.summary: Researchers at IIT Bombay have developed a low-cost and highly efficient solar panel using perovskite technology, which could significantly reduce the cost of solar energy.

articles.headline: New Delhi proposes global framework for AI regulation
articles.summary: India proposed a global framework for AI regulation on Tuesday, emphasizing ethical development and responsible use of artificial intelligence. The proposal seeks international collaboration.

articles.headline: Indian researchers develop new battery technology
articles.summary: Indian researchers have developed a new solid-state battery technology that offers higher energy density and improved safety compared to conventional lithium-ion batteries. The technology could revolutionize energy storage.

articles.headline: Scientists achieve new milestone in quantum computing
articles.summary: Indian scientists have achieved a new milestone in quantum computing by developing a more stable quantum bit (qubit) that can perform complex calculations. This could accelerate the development of quantum computers.

articles.headline: ISRO successfully launches communication satellite
articles.summary: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched a communication satellite on Tuesday, which will enhance telecommunications and broadcasting services. The satellite is placed in geostationary orbit.

articles.headline: India's space program achieves new milestones
articles.summary: India's space program has achieved new milestones with the successful launch of several satellites and the development of advanced space technologies. ISRO continues to push the boundaries of space exploration.

articles.headline: New AI model developed for climate prediction
articles.summary: Researchers have developed a new AI model that can predict climate patterns with greater accuracy, aiding in understanding and mitigating the impacts of climate change. The model utilizes vast datasets.

articles.headline: Scientists develop advanced materials for electronics
articles.summary: Indian researchers have developed advanced materials for next-generation electronics, including high-performance semiconductors and flexible displays. These materials could revolutionize the electronics industry.

articles.headline: India's digital payment system sees record transactions
articles.summary: India's digital payment system recorded a record number of transactions in April, reflecting the growing adoption of digital modes of payment and financial inclusion initiatives.

articles.headline: New technology for faster internet launched
articles.summary: A new technology for faster and more reliable internet connectivity was launched on Tuesday, promising improved bandwidth and reduced latency for users. The technology utilizes advanced network infrastructure.

articles.headline: India strengthens cybersecurity measures
articles.summary: India is strengthening its cybersecurity measures to protect critical infrastructure and sensitive data from cyber threats. New policies and technologies are being implemented.

articles.headline: New AI tool to monitor crop health
articles.summary: A new AI tool has been developed to monitor crop health and predict yield, helping farmers optimize resource use and minimize crop losses. The technology utilizes satellite imagery and machine learning.

articles.headline: New AI platform for education launched
articles.summary: A new AI platform for personalized learning was launched on Tuesday, aiming to enhance educational experiences for students by offering customized content and adaptive learning paths.

articles.headline: New electric vehicle charging stations installed
articles.summary: New electric vehicle (EV) charging stations were installed in major cities on Tuesday, expanding the charging infrastructure to support the growing adoption of EVs. The initiative aims to promote sustainable mobility.

articles.headline: India expands its network of AI research centers
articles.summary: India is expanding its network of AI research centers to foster innovation and develop cutting-edge AI technologies. The initiative aims to make India a global leader in AI.

articles.headline: New cybersecurity framework launched
articles.summary: A new national cybersecurity framework was launched on Tuesday, aimed at enhancing security measures for critical information infrastructure and protecting against cyber threats. The framework provides guidelines for organizations.

articles.headline: New drone technology for surveillance developed
articles.summary: A new drone technology for advanced surveillance and monitoring has been developed by Indian researchers, offering enhanced capabilities for security and disaster management applications.

articles.headline: New AI tool for medical diagnosis released
articles.summary: A new AI tool for medical diagnosis was released on Tuesday, capable of analyzing medical images and assisting doctors in identifying diseases. The tool aims to improve diagnostic accuracy.

articles.headline: New technology for renewable energy storage
articles.summary: A new technology for efficient renewable energy storage has been developed, potentially enabling better integration of solar and wind power into the grid. The technology uses advanced battery systems.

articles.headline: A.P. Anganwadi Centres to Receive 44,346 Induction Stoves
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh CM N. Chandrababu Naidu approved supplying 44,346 energy-efficient induction stoves to Anganwadi Centres through EESL. This Phase-I initiative aims to save ₹24 crore annually in LPG costs and prevent 28,327 tonnes of CO2 emissions, aligning with 'Go Electric' campaign.

articles.headline: UAE Orders Urgent Trial for 10 Over Misleading AI Video
articles.summary: The UAE ordered the arrest and urgent trial of 10 individuals of various nationalities for publishing misleading video clips created using AI. Attorney-General Dr Hamad Saif Al Shamsi said the footage falsely suggested explosions and strikes, prompting swift legal action.

articles.headline: Building AI That Adds Efficiency to Everyday Business Solutions
articles.summary: Dr. Peter Koerte, CTO of Siemens, discusses industrial AI, distinct from regular AI due to its mission-critical, safe, and reliable nature, often trained on engineering data. Siemens aims to make physical infrastructure more productive, with applications like ComfortAI cutting building energy consumption by a third, addressing inefficiencies in facilities.

articles.headline: Next AI Unicorns Will Come from India: Google AI Fund Chief
articles.summary: Jonathan Silber, co-founder of Google AI Futures Fund, believes India will produce the next GenAI unicorns, citing its innovation history. Google partnered with Accel India for a joint investment program, selecting five startups from 4,000 applications. Accel notes a shift towards unique AI applications relevant to the Indian context.

articles.headline: ByteDance Pauses Video AI Tool Launch Amid Copyright Disputes
articles.summary: TikTok’s parent ByteDance has halted the global launch of its video-generation model Seedance 2.0. This follows threats of legal action from Hollywood studios, including Disney, which accused ByteDance of using copyrighted characters to train its AI model and pre-packaging Seedance with a library of such content.

articles.headline: Amazon Picks Cerebras Chips to Run AI Model
articles.summary: Amazon plans to use chips from startup Cerebras Systems alongside its own Trainium processors to run AI software, addressing the voracious demand for AI computing infrastructure. Amazon Web Services will offer a new service based on this arrangement in the second half of 2026.

articles.headline: China's Moonshot AI Valued at $18 Billion After Funding Round
articles.summary: Moonshot AI, the company behind the Kimi chatbot, seeks to raise up to $1 billion in an expanded funding round, valuing the startup at approximately $18 billion. This quadruples its valuation in three months, reflecting growing interest in Chinese AI developers competing with Silicon Valley.

articles.headline: Databricks Ups India Hiring, R&D Spends Amid Growth
articles.summary: Data analytics and AI software company Databricks is aggressively increasing hiring and R&D investments in India, expanding its Bengaluru R&D footprint. With near triple-digit growth in its India business, the company aims to grow its current 1,000-person team and has deployed part of its $250 million India commitment.

articles.headline: Micro-Drama Apps in India Face Monetisation Fear
articles.summary: India’s fast-growing micro-drama segment faces monetisation hurdles as concerns over automatic subscription renewals deter viewers from paid plans. Apps often offer cheap trials but automatically renew subscriptions, prompting calls for more transparent practices. The market, estimated at hundreds of millions, is projected to grow into a multi-billion-dollar category.

articles.headline: AI Edge in Investing: Transforming Portfolio Management and Risk Analysis
articles.summary: AI is transforming investing by enabling faster data analysis, smarter decisions, and high-speed algorithmic trading. Investment firms use AI for risk simulation, sentiment analysis, and automated portfolio rebalancing. While AI offers precision and efficiency, limitations include reliance on historical data and difficulty predicting geopolitical shocks.

articles.headline: Government launches new digital health initiative
articles.summary: The Union Health Ministry launched a new digital health initiative on Tuesday aimed at improving healthcare access and delivery across the country. The program will focus on telemedicine and electronic health records.

articles.headline: India reports 200 new COVID-19 cases
articles.summary: India reported 200 new COVID-19 cases and 5 related deaths on Tuesday. The active caseload stands at 3,500, with a recovery rate of 98.8%.

articles.headline: AIIMS Delhi to expand its services
articles.summary: All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi, announced plans on Tuesday to expand its services and infrastructure to cater to a larger patient population. The expansion will include new wards and advanced medical equipment.

articles.headline: Ministry of AYUSH promotes traditional medicine
articles.summary: The Ministry of AYUSH launched a campaign on Tuesday to promote traditional Indian medicine systems like Ayurveda and Yoga for holistic health and well-being. The campaign targets public awareness.

articles.headline: World Health Organization warns of rising Dengue cases
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) on Tuesday warned of a significant rise in Dengue cases globally, with several countries reporting outbreaks. India is also facing an increase in Dengue infections.

articles.headline: National Health Authority digitizes records
articles.summary: The National Health Authority has digitized over 200 million health records as part of its Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission, aiming to create a comprehensive health information ecosystem for citizens.

articles.headline: AIIMS research finds link between pollution and respiratory diseases
articles.summary: Research conducted at AIIMS Delhi has found a strong link between rising air pollution levels and an increase in respiratory diseases among urban populations. The study highlights the health impact of poor air quality.

articles.headline: Scientists discover potential cure for Alzheimer's
articles.summary: Researchers have discovered a potential cure for Alzheimer's disease in early trials, showing promising results in reversing cognitive decline. The breakthrough offers hope for millions affected by the condition.

articles.headline: National health survey reveals changing health trends
articles.summary: A recently released national health survey revealed changing health trends in India, including a rise in non-communicable diseases and an improvement in life expectancy. The survey provides insights for public health policy.

articles.headline: AIIMS expands telemedicine services
articles.summary: AIIMS has expanded its telemedicine services to reach more patients in remote areas, providing online consultations and medical advice. The initiative aims to improve healthcare accessibility.

articles.headline: WHO issues warning on antimicrobial resistance
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) issued a stern warning on Tuesday about the growing threat of antimicrobial resistance (AMR), urging collective action to combat the spread of drug-resistant infections.

articles.headline: India's healthcare sector to see major investment
articles.summary: India's healthcare sector is set to attract significant investment in the coming years, driven by growing demand for quality medical services and government initiatives to improve healthcare infrastructure.

articles.headline: New vaccine shows promise against infectious diseases
articles.summary: A new vaccine developed by Indian researchers has shown promise in early trials against several infectious diseases, offering potential protection against widespread illnesses. Further clinical trials are planned.

articles.headline: National Health Mission focuses on rural healthcare
articles.summary: The National Health Mission is focusing on improving healthcare access in rural areas by strengthening primary healthcare facilities and expanding telemedicine services. The initiative aims to reach remote populations.

articles.headline: WHO launches campaign against childhood obesity
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) launched a campaign on Tuesday to combat childhood obesity, emphasizing healthy diets and physical activity. The initiative targets improving children's health outcomes globally.

articles.headline: India's healthcare innovation boosted by startup investments
articles.summary: India's healthcare innovation is being boosted by significant startup investments in areas like medical devices, diagnostics, and digital health solutions. The startup ecosystem is driving technological advancements.

articles.headline: New health insurance scheme launched
articles.summary: A new health insurance scheme was launched on Tuesday, providing comprehensive coverage for citizens and aiming to improve access to quality healthcare services. The scheme targets affordability.

articles.headline: WHO warns about surge in vector-borne diseases
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Tuesday about a surge in vector-borne diseases like Dengue and Malaria globally, urging preventive measures and public awareness campaigns.

articles.headline: India's primary healthcare system strengthened
articles.summary: India's primary healthcare system is being strengthened through increased funding and improved infrastructure, aiming to provide accessible and quality healthcare services to all citizens.

articles.headline: WHO stresses importance of vaccination
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) stressed the importance of vaccination on Tuesday, urging global cooperation to ensure equitable access to vaccines and combat infectious diseases.

articles.headline: WHO warns of potential health risks from AI
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) warned on Tuesday about potential health risks associated with Artificial Intelligence (AI), urging responsible development and ethical considerations in AI applications.

articles.headline: WHO launches initiative to combat malnutrition
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) launched a new initiative on Tuesday to combat malnutrition in vulnerable populations, emphasizing the need for improved access to nutritious food and healthcare.

articles.headline: India's pharmaceutical sector shows strong growth
articles.summary: India's pharmaceutical sector has shown strong growth, driven by increased domestic demand and exports of generic medicines. The sector is a key contributor to global healthcare.

articles.headline: WHO urges global action on non-communicable diseases
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) urged global action on non-communicable diseases (NCDs) on Tuesday, highlighting the rising burden of diseases like diabetes and cardiovascular conditions. Prevention and control measures were discussed.

articles.headline: India's health sector focuses on digital transformation
articles.summary: India's health sector is focusing on digital transformation, leveraging technology for telemedicine, electronic health records, and improved healthcare delivery. The aim is to enhance accessibility and efficiency.

articles.headline: ₹23 Crore Dues Cleared for Paediatric Surgeries Under RBSK
articles.summary: State Finance Department approved release of over ₹23 crore in pending dues since 2017 to private hospitals for paediatric surgeries under Rashtriya Bal Swasthya Karyakram (RBSK). The delay was due to a technical issue in procedure listing. The proposal awaits Cabinet approval, bringing relief to hospitals.

articles.headline: Mankind Gearing Up for Early Foray into Semaglutide Market
articles.summary: Mankind Pharma is preparing for an early, independent launch into India’s semaglutide market after the drug’s patent expires this month. Vice Chairman Rajeev Juneja sees a substantial opportunity, given obesity as a growing health crisis, and expects significant price drops with nearly 50 generic brands entering the market.

articles.headline: Challenge Your Brain for Better Cognition and Dementia Prevention
articles.summary: Engaging in diverse hobbies like reading, writing, learning languages, or playing chess can build cognitive reserve and lower Alzheimer’s risk. A study linked lifelong learning to a five-year delay in Alzheimer's onset. Lifestyle changes for heart health, such as exercise and managing blood pressure, also benefit brain health.

articles.headline: Assembly Polls for 4 States & Puducherry Announced; Code of Conduct in Effect
articles.summary: The Election Commission has announced assembly elections for West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry. Polling is scheduled from April 9 to April 29, with results on May 4. West Bengal will have a two-phase poll. The model code of conduct is now in effect across these regions.

articles.headline: Jaishankar Visits Brussels for India-EU FTA Talks
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar is in Brussels for talks with EU counterparts, focusing on advancing the landmark free trade agreement firmed up in January. This is his first foreign visit since the US-Israel-Iran war began, aiming to deepen strategic partnerships and discuss trade.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi Seeks Bharat Ratna for Dalit Leader Kanshi Ram
articles.summary: Rahul Gandhi has urged PM Modi to posthumously award the Bharat Ratna to BSP founder Kanshi Ram, acknowledging his role in empowering the marginalized. BSP chief Mayawati also supported the demand, emphasizing his legacy.

articles.headline: PM Modi Slams Opposition for Disrespecting Lok Sabha Speaker
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi criticized the Opposition's 'dynastic and feudal mindset' after a no-confidence motion against Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla was defeated. He emphasized the difference between disagreement and disrespect, praising Birla's impartiality.

articles.headline: Manipur CM Discusses Security with Amit Shah and NSA Doval
articles.summary: Manipur CM N. Biren Singh met Union Home Minister Amit Shah and NSA Ajit Doval to discuss the state's law and order situation, security measures, and peace restoration efforts.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Reviews Defence Modernization & Procurement
articles.summary: A parliamentary committee reviewed budget allocation for defence, modernization plans, and procurement of new equipment, emphasizing the need to strengthen national security preparedness and timely acquisition of weapon systems.

articles.headline: Bengal CM Announces DA Arrears, Honorarium Hike Before Poll Dates
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee announced payment of DA arrears and a hike in honorarium for religious figures just before the Election Commission declared poll dates. Opposition criticized the move as politically motivated.

articles.headline: NDRF Conducts Mock Drills for Disaster Preparedness
articles.summary: The National Disaster Response Force (NDRF) conducted mock drills across coastal regions to enhance preparedness for natural disasters like cyclones, simulating various emergency scenarios to improve coordination.

articles.headline: Ferrari Launches ₹10.37 Crore Testarossa Amid Supercar Boom
articles.summary: Ferrari launched the 849 Testarossa in Mumbai at ₹10.37 crore, reflecting India's growing ultra-luxury car market. The supercar segment sees over 1,000 sales annually, with UHNWI population projected to grow 50% by 2028.

articles.headline: upGrad Plans All-Stock Acquisition of Edtech Rival Unacademy
articles.summary: Upskilling firm upGrad is set to acquire Unacademy in an all-stock deal. Unacademy CEO Gaurav Munjal will remain CEO. This follows Unacademy's refocusing and precedes an ESOP buyback.

articles.headline: India's Manufacturing PMI Rises to 54.6 in May
articles.summary: India's manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) increased to 54.6 in May, signaling sustained expansion driven by new orders and increased production. The sector shows continued growth.

articles.headline: India's Startup Ecosystem Attracts Strong Venture Funding
articles.summary: India's startup ecosystem continues to attract significant venture capital, with investments rising in early and growth-stage companies, particularly in fintech, e-commerce, and SaaS.

articles.headline: India's Services Sector Maintains Robust Growth Momentum
articles.summary: India's services sector showed strong growth in May, driven by demand in IT, finance, and hospitality. This sector is a crucial contributor to the country's economic expansion.

articles.headline: Indian Tanker Jag Laadki Safely Exits Fujairah Post-Attack
articles.summary: The Indian-flagged tanker Jag Laadki safely left Fujairah, UAE, after a drone attack, carrying crude oil. The government confirmed all Indian vessels in the region are monitored, and refineries have adequate stock.

articles.headline: LPG Bookings Drop as Panic Buying Eases; Supply Stable
articles.summary: LPG refill bookings fell as panic buying subsided following government assurances of stable supply. Refineries are operating at high capacity, and states are preventing hoarding.

articles.headline: ADB Projects India's GDP Growth at 6.3% for FY24
articles.summary: The Asian Development Bank (ADB) forecasts India's GDP growth at 6.3% for FY24, citing resilience despite global economic uncertainties driven by strong domestic demand.

articles.headline: India's Merchandise Exports Rise 8.3% in April
articles.summary: India's merchandise exports grew by 8.3% year-on-year in April, reaching $34.6 billion, boosted by sectors like engineering goods and petroleum products.

articles.headline: India's Trade Deficit Narrows in April
articles.summary: India's trade deficit decreased to $15 billion in April, down from $16.5 billion in March, due to increased exports and reduced imports, signaling a positive trade trend.

articles.headline: Adani Power Wins 1,600 MW Contract for Maharashtra Supply
articles.summary: Adani Power secured a 25-year contract to supply 1,600 MW of power to Maharashtra starting FY31, having been the lowest bidder with a tariff of ₹5.3 per kWh.

articles.headline: Indian Railways to Upgrade 1,000 Stations
articles.summary: Indian Railways plans to upgrade 1,000 stations nationwide, enhancing passenger amenities, Wi-Fi, waiting rooms, and food services to improve the travel experience.

articles.headline: New Railway Line & Metro Line Projects Inaugurated
articles.summary: New railway and metro line projects were inaugurated, aiming to improve passenger and freight transport, boost regional development, and enhance public transportation and connectivity.

articles.headline: India Accelerates Infrastructure Development with Significant Funding
articles.summary: India's infrastructure development, including road, rail, and power projects, is accelerating with significant funding, prioritizing economic growth and national connectivity.

articles.headline: India Hosts Climate Change Conference, Promotes Renewable Energy
articles.summary: India hosted an international conference on climate change and renewable energy, emphasizing collective action, clean technologies, solar and wind power, and fostering global partnerships.

articles.headline: India Strengthens Climate Action Cooperation with France
articles.summary: India and France agreed to enhance cooperation on climate action, focusing on renewable energy and sustainable practices to accelerate climate goals and lead global efforts.

articles.headline: India's Renewable Energy Capacity Crosses 180 GW
articles.summary: India's renewable energy capacity has surpassed 180 GW, with solar and wind power leading the growth. The nation is committed to its clean energy targets.

articles.headline: TDP MP, Ex-MLA Detained in Hyderabad Drug Bust
articles.summary: TDP MP Putta Mahesh Kumar and ex-BRS MLA Pilot Rohith Reddy were detained after cocaine was found at a Hyderabad farmhouse. Six tested positive for narcotics; one allegedly fired a revolver.

articles.headline: RCom Challenges SC Ruling on Spectrum as Asset in Insolvency
articles.summary: Reliance Communications filed a review petition against the Supreme Court's ruling that spectrum cannot be an asset in insolvency, arguing it's crucial for telecom financing and the IBC's workability.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Issues Notices on PILs for UCC and Electoral Reforms
articles.summary: The Supreme Court issued notices to governments on PILs seeking Uniform Civil Code and electoral reforms, aiming for gender justice, national integration, and transparency in political funding.

articles.headline: Manipur Violence: SC Hears Plea for Central Intervention and Accountability
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is hearing Public Interest Litigations seeking central intervention to restore peace in Manipur and demanding accountability for the violence, emphasizing justice for victims.

articles.headline: SC Hears Pleas Against Disqualification of MPs and MLAs
articles.summary: The Supreme Court heard petitions concerning the disqualification of MPs for alleged misconduct and MLAs for anti-defection violations, seeking responses from concerned parties.

articles.headline: SC Issues Notices on Plea for Political Funding Transparency, Data Protection
articles.summary: The Supreme Court issued notices on pleas seeking transparency in political funding and stronger data protection laws, aiming to safeguard citizens' personal information.

articles.headline: CBSE Cancels Class 12 Exams in Gulf Nations Due to West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: The CBSE cancelled Class 12 board exams for students in Bahrain, Iran, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and UAE due to the escalating Iran-US-Israel conflict, impacting thousands.

articles.headline: Indian Classical Music Concert Held in New Delhi
articles.summary: A concert celebrating India's rich classical music heritage was held in New Delhi, featuring renowned artists and attracting a large audience.

articles.headline: National Festival Celebrates India's Diverse Folk Traditions
articles.summary: A national festival showcasing India's diverse folk traditions concluded, featuring music, dance, and art forms to preserve cultural heritage and promote national integration.

articles.headline: North Korea Tests Nuclear-Capable Rocket Launchers Amid Military Drills
articles.summary: Kim Jong Un oversaw tests of nuclear-capable rocket launchers, a day after North Korea detected missile launches. This occurs amid joint US-South Korea military drills.

articles.headline: Ukraine Offers Drone Defense Tech to West Asia for Aid
articles.summary: President Zelenskyy stated Ukraine will share drone defense technology with West Asian nations for money and tech, demonstrating capabilities against Iranian drones.

articles.headline: Russia-Ukraine War: Diplomatic Efforts Continue Amid Stalled Talks
articles.summary: Diplomatic efforts to resolve the Russia-Ukraine conflict persist, though peace talks remain stalled. International mediation continues amidst ongoing hostilities.

articles.headline: India and Maldives/Russia Discuss Defence Cooperation
articles.summary: India held discussions with Maldives and Russia to strengthen defence cooperation, maritime security, joint patrols, and technology transfer, aiming to bolster strategic ties.

articles.headline: Russia-Ukraine Conflict: Diplomatic Talks Face Challenges
articles.summary: Diplomatic efforts to resolve the Russia-Ukraine conflict face challenges due to ongoing disagreements, though international mediation for a ceasefire continues.

articles.headline: India-China Border Talks Held to Disengage Troops
articles.summary: India and China held diplomatic talks to discuss troop disengagement along the Line of Actual Control (LAC) and restore peace in border areas, reporting progress.

articles.headline: Gill & Mandhana Bag Top BCCI International Cricketer Awards
articles.summary: Shubman Gill and Smriti Mandhana won Best International Cricketer awards at the BCCI Naman Awards. Rahul Dravid, Mithali Raj, and Roger Binny received Lifetime Achievement awards.

articles.headline: Gulveer Singh Breaks National Half Marathon Record in NYC
articles.summary: Gulveer Singh finished third at the New York City Half Marathon, setting a new Indian national record with a time of 59:42, surpassing Avinash Sable's record.

articles.headline: Kimi Antonelli Wins Chinese GP, Becomes Second-Youngest F1 Winner
articles.summary: 19-year-old Kimi Antonelli secured his maiden F1 victory at the Chinese Grand Prix, leading a Mercedes 1-2 finish. Max Verstappen was the only younger winner.

articles.headline: Bahrain, Saudi F1 Races Cancelled Due to West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: Formula 1 Grands Prix in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia have been cancelled in April due to the ongoing West Asia war, with no replacement races scheduled.

articles.headline: Scientists Develop Efficient Solar Energy Storage System
articles.summary: Indian scientists have developed an efficient, low-cost solar energy storage system using advanced battery technology to address the intermittency of solar power.

articles.headline: India Enhances Healthcare Infrastructure, Focus on Rural Areas
articles.summary: India is enhancing healthcare infrastructure by establishing new hospitals and upgrading facilities, with a focus on rural areas, to improve access to medical services.
